name: JD Coinlink Technology HK
summary: JD Coinlink Technology Hong Kong Limited is a subsidiary of China's e-commerce giant JD.com. The entity applied for inclusion in the HKMA Stablecoin Issuer Sandbox in 2024 and subsequently submitted a formal application under the Stablecoins Ordinance. The proposed stablecoin (JDSC) would be pegged 1:1 to the Hong Kong Dollar with reserves held in HKD-denominated bank deposits and short-dated HKSAR government debt.

capital_adequacy.label: Capital Adequacy
capital_adequacy.rationale: The HKMA has not publicly disclosed minimum paid-up capital figures for sandbox participants. JD.com's group-level financial strength is well-documented, but the ring-fenced capital of the HK issuer entity has not been confirmed in public filings.

reserve_quality.label: Reserve Quality
reserve_quality.rationale: Reserve composition and custodian arrangements have not been publicly attested. The sandbox framework requires reserves to be fully backed, but independent attestation reports have not been published.

governance.label: Governance
governance.rationale: Board composition and compliance officer appointments for the Hong Kong issuer entity have not been made public. Parent company JD.com operates under PRC and US listing governance standards, but the subsidiary's standalone governance framework is undisclosed.

technology.label: Technology & Custody
technology.rationale: The smart contract codebase for JDSC has not been publicly released or audited by a named third party. Custody arrangements (whether self-custodied or third-party) are undisclosed.

redemption.label: Redemption Mechanics
redemption.rationale: Par-value redemption commitment and minimum redemption amounts have not been publicly specified. The sandbox phase is intended to test operational readiness before public issuance.

disclosure.label: Public Disclosure
disclosure.rationale: No periodic reserve attestation or transparency reports have been published for JDSC. The HKMA sandbox terms require participants to submit reports to the regulator, but public disclosure obligations have not yet taken effect.

reserve_details: Proposed: 100% HKD bank deposits and short-dated HKSAR government securities. Custodian TBC. No public attestation available as of review date.
governance_notes: Parent: JD.com, Inc. (NASDAQ: JD; HKEX: 9618). The HK issuer entity is a wholly-owned subsidiary. Independent director appointments and AML/CFT compliance officer identity not disclosed.
technology_notes: Blockchain platform not publicly confirmed. No published smart contract audit. JD.com operates blockchain infrastructure (JD Chain) but JDSC's deployment chain is unspecified.
redemption_notes: Intended 1:1 HKD redemption. Minimum redemption threshold, processing time (T+0 vs T+1), and business-hours restrictions not yet published.
disclosure_notes: No public reserve attestation as of April 2026. Subject to HKMA reporting requirements under the Stablecoins Ordinance once licensed.

All SARM dimensions are gray (insufficient public disclosure) pending sandbox completion and formal licensing. name: Anchorpoint Financial
parent: Standard Chartered Bank (HK) / Animoca Brands / HKT
summary: Anchorpoint Financial is a joint venture between Standard Chartered Bank (Hong Kong), Animoca Brands, and Hong Kong Telecommunications (HKT). It received one of the first two stablecoin issuer licences issued by the HKMA under the Stablecoins Ordinance (Cap. 656). Its product, HKDAP (HKD At Par), is a HKD-pegged stablecoin intended to be distributed via a B2B2C model through authorised distribution partners, with use cases including settlement of tokenised real-world assets.

capital_adequacy.label: Capital Adequacy
capital_adequacy.rationale: The issuer SPV's capitalization has not been disclosed. Standard Chartered HK (licensed deposit-taking institution) provides implicit financial backing, but the ring-fenced issuer entity's own capital position is unconfirmed.

reserve_quality.label: Reserve Quality
reserve_quality.rationale: Anchorpoint Financial is backed by Standard Chartered Bank (Hong Kong) Limited, one of Hong Kong's three note-issuing banks, which provides custody and trust arrangements for reserve assets. HKDAP is backed 1:1 by high-quality HKD assets held in trust under Cap. 656 requirements, segregated from Anchorpoint's operating funds. Standard Chartered's governance framework ensures reserve segregation, custody control, and reliable redemption. Monthly reserve attestation reports are filed with the HKMA but specific asset composition remains pending public disclosure ahead of the Q2 2026 phased launch.

The entity is subject to the HKMA's Guideline on AML/CFT for Licensed Stablecoin Issuers. Standard Chartered, as largest shareholder, provides custodial oversight, compliance control, and redemption procedure governance. Specific roles and reporting lines for stablecoin operations (CRO, CCO, CTO) are pending public disclosure. Travel Rule implementation and blockchain analytics partnerships are under development ahead of the Q2 2026 launch.

name: Ant Group
summary: Ant Group's digital finance unit (Ant International) has applied for a stablecoin issuer licence in Hong Kong. Ant International is a subsidiary of Ant Group, a global financial technology leader and operator of Alipay (10+ billion users). The entity reportedly participated in the HKMA's regulatory sandbox in 2024. However, Ant remains subject to significant geopolitical constraints imposed by the PBOC, including a paused Hong Kong pilot (September 2025) and the Yinfa No. 42 directive restricting CNH-pegged stablecoins (February 2026). As of May 2026, Ant has not received a stablecoin issuer licence.

reserve_quality.rationale: Ant Group's digital finance unit (Ant International) has applied for a stablecoin issuer licence in Hong Kong but has not yet received approval as of May 2026. While Ant Group is a global financial technology leader with institutional backing and professional treasury operations at scale, Hong Kong-specific reserve composition, custody arrangements, and segregation procedures for any stablecoin remain pending public disclosure. The PBOC's Yinfa No. 42 directive (February 2026) restricting CNH-pegged stablecoins may influence Ant's product strategy but does not preclude HKD or USD stablecoin issuance. SARM assessment will be updated if a licence is granted and operational disclosures are made available.

Additionally, regulatory constraints from the PBOC (Yinfa No. 42 and paused HK pilot in September 2025) create additional governance and compliance complexity that may influence licence conditions or timeline.

reviewer_note:
Ant Group (through Ant International) applied for a stablecoin issuer licence under the Stablecoins Ordinance (Cap. 656) and remains under HKMA review as of May 2026. The entity reportedly participated in the HKMA's regulatory sandbox in 2024 [sandbox participation requires fact-check against primary HKMA disclosure] but did not receive a licence in the first batch announced on April 10, 2026.

**Regulatory Status**: Under review; licence decision pending (estimated 2026-Q2 or later).

**Geopolitical Risk**: High. The PBOC issued Yinfa No. 42 (February 2026) restricting CNH-pegged stablecoins and previously ordered Ant to pause its Hong Kong stablecoin pilot (September 2025). These constraints may limit Ant's product options (HKD or USD instead of CNH) and could delay or prevent licence issuance. Ant's March 2026 acquisition of Yau Choy Securities suggests continued strategic intent despite PBOC constraints.

**SARM Assessment**: All dimensions assessed as "gray" (insufficient public data). Ant possesses global scale, institutional governance, and AML/CFT experience, but Hong Kong-specific operational disclosures and the resolution of geopolitical constraints are pending.

**Key Question**: Will the HKMA licence a mainland-backed fintech firm despite PBOC constraints? Ant's licensing outcome will be a key indicator of HKMA's regulatory independence and appetite for geopolitical complexity.

Source: HKMA press releases, media reports (Substack, BitcoinEthereumNews, CryptoNews, Sumsub), regulatory guidance documents.

name: Circle (EURC)
summary: Euro-denominated e-money token issued by Circle Mint France SAS under a French ACPR Electronic Money Institution licence, passporting across the EEA under MiCA's E-Money Token (EMT) regime. 1:1 backed by euro-denominated cash and short-term reserves; redeemable at par.

capital_adequacy.label: Capital Adequacy
capital_adequacy.rationale: Circle Mint France SAS holds a French EMI licence requiring minimum own funds of EUR 350,000 plus ongoing capital proportional to outstanding e-money under Directive 2009/110/EC. MiCA Article 35 imposes additional 2% of average outstanding e-money requirement on significant EMT issuers. Parent Circle Internet Financial has disclosed capital position via SEC filings.

reserve_quality.label: Reserve Quality
reserve_quality.rationale: Reserves segregated at EU credit institutions plus Circle Reserve Fund (BlackRock-managed government MMF holding short-dated US Treasuries). MiCA requires 30% (60% for significant issuers) at credit institutions, remainder in highly liquid government instruments. Monthly attestations by Deloitte verify 1:1 backing.

governance.label: Governance
governance.rationale: Multi-jurisdiction regulatory oversight: NYDFS BitLicense and limited-purpose trust charter (US), FCA EMI registration (UK), ACPR EMI licence (France). Senior management subject to ACPR fit-and-proper assessment. Independent board members; documented internal control framework.

technology.label: Technology & Custody
technology.rationale: EURC deployed natively on Ethereum, Avalanche, Solana, Base, and Stellar with audited smart contracts (ChainSecurity, Halborn). Role-based access controls for mint/burn/blocklist functions. Reserve custody at regulated EU credit institutions and SEC-registered MMF. HSM-based key management.

redemption.label: Redemption Mechanics
redemption.rationale: MiCA Article 39 mandates redemption at par at any time without fees. Institutional Circle Mint customers (KYC-verified) redeem 1:1 against EUR deposits on T+1. Retail holders access redemption via exchanges and Circle Mint partners. No redemption fees permitted.

disclosure.label: Public Disclosure
disclosure.rationale: Monthly third-party attestations by Deloitte per AICPA AT-C 105 standards. Weekly reserve composition reports with Treasury CUSIP-level holdings. Whitepaper notified to ACPR per MiCA Article 51. Ongoing prudential reporting to ACPR. Annual audited group financials.

reserve_details: Reserves held in segregated accounts at EU credit institutions plus the Circle Reserve Fund — a SEC-registered government money market fund managed by BlackRock investing exclusively in short-dated US Treasuries (typically <3 months), repos collateralised by Treasuries, and cash. MiCA reserve segregation rules apply with bankruptcy-remote treatment under French law.
governance_notes: French SAS structure with ACPR-approved senior management. Group-level oversight via Circle Internet Financial, subject to NYDFS supervision in the US. Independent board members; compliance function with direct reporting line to senior management. Internal audit and risk management functions separated per ACPR requirements.
technology_notes: Multi-chain native deployment (not bridged) on Ethereum, Solana, Avalanche, Base, and Stellar. Contracts include role-based controls for minting, burning, and blacklisting addresses subject to EU sanctions. Open source contract code; periodic third-party audits.
redemption_notes: Two-tier redemption: institutional Circle Mint customers redeem directly 1:1 with T+1 settlement; retail users redeem via secondary markets or Circle Mint partners. MiCA prohibits redemption fees. Cross-stablecoin swap available between EURC and USDC.
disclosure_notes: Reserve composition published weekly with CUSIP-level Treasury holdings. Monthly attestation by Deloitte. ACPR receives ongoing prudential reporting. Significant-issuer thresholds under MiCA (10M users or EUR 5B in circulation) would trigger enhanced EBA supervision; EURC currently below thresholds.
reviewer_note: Pilot entry for global expansion. SARM signals reflect public information; structural assessment under MiCA EMT regime. EURC market cap remains modest relative to USDC; significant-issuer thresholds not triggered.

name: StraitsX (XSGD)
summary: Singapore-dollar e-money token issued by StraitsX Pte. Ltd., the first stablecoin issuer to receive MAS Major Payment Institution licence with permission to issue Single-Currency Stablecoins (SCS) under the MAS SCS Framework. 1:1 backed by SGD cash and short-term reserves; redeemable at par within 5 business days.

capital_adequacy.label: Capital Adequacy
capital_adequacy.rationale: MAS SCS Framework requires base capital of SGD 1 million or 50% of annual operating expenses (whichever higher). StraitsX holds a Major Payment Institution (MPI) licence under the Payment Services Act with explicit SCS issuance scope. Parent Fazz Financial Group provides additional group-level capital support.

sources.label: MAS SCS Framework final response paper (Aug 2023)

reserve_quality.label: Reserve Quality
reserve_quality.rationale: Reserves held in segregated accounts at MAS-licensed banks (DBS, Standard Chartered). MAS SCS Framework permits cash, cash equivalents, and debt securities with residual maturity ≤3 months issued by central banks or sovereign issuers with credit rating ≥AA-. Monthly attestations by independent auditor (KPMG); reserve composition publicly disclosed.

governance.label: Governance
governance.rationale: MPI licence requires MAS fit-and-proper assessment of CEO, directors, and substantial shareholders. Documented risk management framework covering operational, technology, and compliance risks. Group-level oversight via Fazz Financial Group with regional regulatory footprint (Indonesia OJK, Singapore MAS).

sources.label: MAS Payment Services Act licensee obligations

technology.label: Technology & Custody
technology.rationale: XSGD deployed on Ethereum, Polygon, Avalanche, Hedera, and Zilliqa with audited smart contracts. Multi-signature controls for mint/burn operations. Reserve custody at MAS-licensed banks; smart contracts include freeze and blacklist functions for compliance enforcement.

redemption.label: Redemption Mechanics
redemption.rationale: MAS SCS Framework mandates par redemption within 5 business days of request. StraitsX customers redeem 1:1 against SGD bank deposits via StraitsX platform. Institutional customers access faster settlement; retail redemption subject to KYC verification. No redemption fees per MAS rules.

disclosure.label: Public Disclosure
disclosure.rationale: Monthly third-party attestations by KPMG covering reserve composition and value. Public transparency portal with real-time circulation data. MAS-regulated label restricted to compliant issuers; StraitsX displays MAS-regulated marker on official channels. Whitepaper notified to MAS.

reserve_details: Reserves held in segregated trust accounts at MAS-licensed banks (DBS Bank, Standard Chartered Bank Singapore). Composition limited to SGD cash, MAS bills, and Singapore Government Securities with residual maturity ≤3 months per SCS Framework. Bankruptcy-remote treatment under Singapore trust law.
governance_notes: Singapore-incorporated entity under Fazz Financial Group. CEO and directors subject to MAS fit-and-proper review. Three-lines-of-defence risk framework with separate compliance and internal audit functions. Substantial shareholders disclosed to MAS per PS Act requirements.
technology_notes: Native deployment across multiple chains with consistent contract architecture. Mint/burn controlled by multi-sig wallets under operational segregation. Smart contracts include compliance-driven freeze and blacklist functions activatable by StraitsX compliance team.
redemption_notes: Two-tier redemption: institutional clients via direct StraitsX integration with same-day or T+1 settlement; retail users via StraitsX consumer app with redemption within 5 business days per MAS SCS rules. Cross-currency conversion available between XSGD and XUSD.
disclosure_notes: Reserve composition published with bank-level detail. Monthly KPMG attestations per AT-C 105. MAS receives ongoing prudential reporting including liquidity and capital metrics. Public transparency portal updated monthly.
reviewer_note: First stablecoin issuer to receive MAS Major Payment Institution licence with SCS issuance scope under the Single-Currency Stablecoin Framework. Reference case for the Asian SCS-style regime.

name: SG-Forge (EURCV)
summary: Euro-denominated e-money token issued by Société Générale-FORGE, a regulated subsidiary of Société Générale group. First bank-issued EMT under MiCA, building on SG-FORGE's prior credit institution status. Issued natively on public Ethereum and accessible to both institutional and retail users since late 2024.

capital_adequacy.label: Capital Adequacy
capital_adequacy.rationale: SG-FORGE operates under a French credit institution licence (ACPR-authorised), subject to CRR/CRD IV capital adequacy requirements far exceeding EMI thresholds. MiCA Article 35 own funds requirement (2% of average outstanding e-money for significant issuers) is comfortably met. Group-level support from Société Générale (G-SIB) provides additional structural buffer.

reserve_quality.label: Reserve Quality
reserve_quality.rationale: Reserves held on the balance sheet of Société Générale group (G-SIB, ECB-supervised) under credit institution status — providing reserve quality structurally stronger than non-bank EMT issuers since deposits are protected by CRR liquidity requirements. MiCA segregation rules ensure bankruptcy-remote treatment of token-holder claims.

governance.label: Governance
governance.rationale: SG-FORGE governance integrated with Société Générale group risk and compliance frameworks. Subject to ECB direct supervision under SSM via parent. Independent risk management and internal audit functions. Senior management subject to ACPR fit-and-proper review plus group-level oversight.

technology.label: Technology & Custody
technology.rationale: EURCV deployed natively on Ethereum mainnet with publicly audited smart contracts. Token contract supports allowlist-based transfers (institutional version) and open transfers (retail version post-MiCA). Cryptographic key management under bank-grade HSM infrastructure. Smart contract upgrade controls via multi-sig governance.

redemption.label: Redemption Mechanics
redemption.rationale: MiCA Article 39 par-redemption right applies. SG-FORGE customers redeem 1:1 against EUR bank account credits. Bank-issuer model provides natural redemption pathway through existing banking infrastructure. No redemption fees per MiCA. Retail-accessible redemption since late 2024 expansion.

disclosure.label: Public Disclosure
disclosure.rationale: Group-level Pillar 3 disclosures under CRR apply to SG-FORGE as part of Société Générale consolidated reporting. Whitepaper notified to ACPR per MiCA Article 51. EURCV circulation data published on-chain (Ethereum public ledger) provides real-time transparency. Annual audited group financials.

reserve_details: Reserves backed by deposits held on Société Générale group balance sheet — the bank-issuer model under MiCA differs structurally from non-bank EMI issuers (Circle, Banking Circle). Token-holder claims rank as e-money obligations with MiCA segregation protections; in resolution scenarios, bank resolution regime (BRRD) applies in parallel.
governance_notes: Wholly-owned subsidiary of Société Générale, operating under group risk appetite framework. Board composition includes group-appointed and independent directors. Compliance function integrated with parent's AML/CFT, sanctions, and conduct programmes. ECB SSM oversight via parent G-SIB status.
technology_notes: EURCV issued natively on Ethereum mainnet (no bridging from off-chain ledger). Two contract versions historically: institutional allowlist version and retail-accessible version. Bank-controlled mint/burn with HSM-protected keys. Smart contract code published and audited.
redemption_notes: Bank-issuer redemption advantage: holders redeem via standard EUR bank credit, leveraging Société Générale group payment rails. Institutional customers via direct SG-FORGE platform; retail customers via partner integrations and exchanges.
disclosure_notes: EURCV on-chain circulation visible via Ethereum block explorers. Group-level disclosures under CRR Pillar 3 framework provide structural transparency. ACPR receives ongoing supervisory reporting via banking regime; MiCA-specific reporting layered on top.

citations.label: EBA guidance on MiCA Title III

reviewer_note: Bank-issuer EMT under MiCA — structurally distinct from EMI-issuer model (Circle). Reference case for credit institution issuance route under MiCA Title III. CRR/CRD IV regime applies alongside MiCA, creating dual-layer prudential oversight.

name: JPYC
summary: Japanese-yen prepaid-type stablecoin issued by JPYC Inc. Currently operating as a prepaid payment instrument under the Payment Services Act; has applied for Type II Fund Transfer Service Provider registration to transition to the 2023 revised PSA stablecoin regime that enables redemption-at-par electronic payment instrument status.

capital_adequacy.label: Capital Adequacy
capital_adequacy.rationale: Currently operates under prepaid payment instrument regime with FSA registration but lower capital requirements than the Type II Fund Transfer Service Provider status it is applying for. Capital position smaller than bank or trust company issuers; transition to FTSP regime would impose stricter requirements. Independent issuer without group-level capital support comparable to bank-issued alternatives (Progmat Coin model).

reserve_quality.label: Reserve Quality
reserve_quality.rationale: Reserves held as bank deposits and Japanese Government Bonds per prepaid payment instrument rules. Under current regime, only 50% asset preservation required (the prepaid regime — not the stablecoin regime). Transition to FTSP status would require 100% backing in qualifying assets. Public reserve transparency limited compared to MiCA/MAS-regulated peers.

governance.label: Governance
governance.rationale: Independent fintech issuer; smaller scale governance compared to bank or trust-based issuers under the revised PSA. FSA registration as prepaid payment instrument issuer requires fit-and-proper assessment of representative director and key personnel. Pending FTSP application would impose enhanced governance standards.

technology.label: Technology & Custody
technology.rationale: JPYC deployed on Ethereum, Polygon, Avalanche, Astar, and Shiden with verified contracts. ERC-20 standard with mint/burn controls. Public chain deployment provides transparency on circulation. Reserve custody at Japanese banks under FSA prepaid regime asset preservation rules.

redemption.label: Redemption Mechanics
redemption.rationale: Under current prepaid payment instrument regime, JPYC is NOT redeemable at par for fiat cash — a structural limitation distinguishing it from bank/trust-issued stablecoins under the revised PSA. Holders can only use JPYC for goods/services within accepted merchant network or exchange via secondary markets. Par-redemption status pending FTSP registration approval.

sources.label: JFSA prepaid payment instrument rules — no cash redemption

disclosure.label: Public Disclosure
disclosure.rationale: Periodic reserve attestations published but less frequent and less detailed than MiCA-regulated peers. FSA receives prepaid issuer regulatory reporting. On-chain circulation observable via public blockchains. Transparency would significantly improve under pending FTSP status given enhanced disclosure obligations.

reserve_details: Operates under prepaid payment instrument regime requiring 50% asset preservation rather than 100% backing. Reserve held as bank deposits and JGBs at Japanese financial institutions. Transition to Type II FTSP would require 100% backing in qualifying liquid assets and trust segregation.
governance_notes: Tokyo-incorporated independent fintech issuer. FSA-registered prepaid payment instrument issuer with representative director and key personnel subject to fit-and-proper review. Pending application for Type II Fund Transfer Service Provider registration would impose enhanced governance, risk management, and capital requirements.
technology_notes: Multi-chain deployment on Ethereum, Polygon, Avalanche, Astar, and Shiden using standard ERC-20 contracts. Mint/burn controlled by JPYC operational keys. Public on-chain circulation; smart contracts verified on respective block explorers.
redemption_notes: Critical structural limitation: prepaid payment instrument status under Japanese law explicitly excludes par-redemption for cash. Holders use JPYC for in-network payments or exchange via secondary markets. This distinguishes JPYC from the bank/trust-issued stablecoin model that the 2023 PSA amendments enabled (e.g. Progmat Coin). Par-redemption status contingent on FTSP approval.
disclosure_notes: Periodic reserve disclosures less detailed than MiCA/MAS-regulated peers. FSA receives prepaid issuer regulatory reporting. Transition to FTSP regime would substantially upgrade public transparency obligations.
reviewer_note: Reference case for Japan's bifurcated stablecoin landscape: prepaid-type fintech issuers (JPYC) vs bank/trust-issued stablecoins under revised PSA (Progmat Coin). Structural redemption gap is regime-driven, not issuer-specific. Pending FTSP application would materially upgrade SARM signals across multiple dimensions.

name: Progmat Coin
parent: Progmat, Inc. (Mitsubishi UFJ Trust and Banking)
summary: Trust-type stablecoin infrastructure operated by Progmat, Inc. (Mitsubishi UFJ Trust spin-off) under the 2023 revised Payment Services Act. Enables licensed banks and trust companies to issue JPY-pegged stablecoins backed by trust beneficiary rights. First major deployment: GMO Aozora Net Bank issuance via Progmat platform.

capital_adequacy.label: Capital Adequacy
capital_adequacy.rationale: Issuance restricted to FSA-licensed banks, trust companies, or fund transfer service providers under the 2023 revised PSA — all subject to banking-equivalent capital requirements. Progmat itself is operated by MUFG Trust subsidiary with group-level capital backing. Trust structure provides bankruptcy-remote separation between issuer entity and stablecoin reserves.

reserve_quality.label: Reserve Quality
reserve_quality.rationale: Trust-type stablecoins backed 100% by JPY deposits held in segregated trust accounts at MUFG Trust Bank or equivalent licensed trust banks. Trust structure provides legal segregation under Japanese trust law — token holders hold beneficiary rights, not unsecured claims. Asset preservation requirement is 100% under revised PSA, materially stronger than prepaid issuer regime.

governance.label: Governance
governance.rationale: MUFG group-level governance and risk management apply to Progmat platform. Issuing entities (banks/trust companies) operate under FSA banking or trust company supervision. Multi-party governance: platform operator (Progmat), issuing financial institution, and trust bank holding reserves. Senior management subject to FSA fit-and-proper assessment via banking regime.

technology.label: Technology & Custody
technology.rationale: Progmat Coin platform supports issuance on multiple blockchains (Polygon, Avalanche, Cosmos, R3 Corda, ethereum). Permissioned-access architecture with KYC-integrated transfer controls. Reserve custody at licensed trust banks under Japanese trust law. Smart contract architecture audited; platform-level operational controls under MUFG group security standards.

redemption.label: Redemption Mechanics
redemption.rationale: Trust-type structure provides clear par-redemption right under Japanese trust law. Token holders redeem 1:1 against JPY bank deposits via issuing financial institution. Banking-rail redemption infrastructure ensures settlement reliability. Materially different from JPYC prepaid model which lacks cash-redemption right.

disclosure.label: Public Disclosure
disclosure.rationale: Issuing financial institutions subject to standard FSA banking/trust disclosures. Trust-account reserve transparency strong via trust bank statements but public-facing real-time transparency portal less developed than MiCA peers. Progmat platform publishes architecture documentation; per-token public attestation cadence varies by issuing institution.

reserve_details: 100% backing in JPY bank deposits segregated in trust accounts at licensed Japanese trust banks (primarily MUFG Trust). Trust structure provides bankruptcy-remote treatment under Japanese Trust Act — token holders hold beneficiary rights with priority over general creditors of the issuing institution. Structural advantage over both prepaid (JPYC) and EMI-type (Circle EU) models in resolution scenarios.
governance_notes: Multi-party governance model: Progmat, Inc. (MUFG Trust subsidiary) operates the platform; FSA-licensed banks or trust companies act as issuers; trust banks hold reserves. MUFG group risk management and compliance frameworks apply to platform operations. Issuers subject to FSA banking or trust company supervision.
technology_notes: Multi-chain platform: Polygon, Avalanche, Cosmos, Corda, Ethereum support. Permissioned-access via KYC integration with issuing financial institutions. Smart contracts include transfer restrictions enforcing AML/CFT requirements. Reserve custody managed at trust bank level with conventional banking custody standards.
redemption_notes: Trust-beneficiary redemption right under Japanese law provides par-redemption certainty. Settlement via banking rails leveraging issuing financial institution's existing payment infrastructure. Same-day or T+1 settlement typical for institutional users; retail redemption channels depend on specific issuer.
disclosure_notes: Standard FSA banking and trust disclosure framework applies. Public real-time transparency portal less developed than MiCA peers but trust-account reserve verification structurally robust. Periodic attestations vary by issuing financial institution.

citations.label: GMO Aozora Net Bank stablecoin announcement

reviewer_note: Reference case for Japan's bank/trust-issuer stablecoin model under the revised PSA — structurally distinct from prepaid-type issuers (JPYC). The bifurcation between trust-type and prepaid-type is the defining feature of Japan's regulatory architecture and a useful comparator for jurisdictions designing hybrid regimes.

name: AE Coin
summary: AED-pegged stablecoin issued by AED Stablecoin LLC under in-principle approval from the Central Bank of the UAE (CBUAE) under the Payment Token Services Regulation (2024). First AED-referenced regulated stablecoin; targets domestic UAE payment use cases and dirham-denominated tokenized asset settlement.

capital_adequacy.label: Capital Adequacy
capital_adequacy.rationale: CBUAE Payment Token Services Regulation imposes minimum capital requirements on licensed issuers, with detailed thresholds published in supporting CBUAE rulebooks. AED Stablecoin LLC is an independent issuer without group-level bank backing; capital adequacy assessment limited by absence of public financial disclosures comparable to MiCA/MAS-regulated peers. Regime is new (2024), implementation precedents still developing.

reserve_quality.label: Reserve Quality
reserve_quality.rationale: CBUAE rules require 100% backing in AED-denominated cash and qualifying liquid assets held at UAE-licensed banks. AE Coin reserves reportedly held at major UAE banks. However, public reserve composition disclosures are less granular than MiCA/MAS peers, and independent third-party attestation cadence not yet established publicly. Reserve quality contingent on ongoing CBUAE supervisory enforcement.

governance.label: Governance
governance.rationale: CBUAE in-principle approval requires fit-and-proper assessment of senior management and substantial shareholders. Independent issuer structure without integration into established banking group governance frameworks. Risk management and compliance functions documented at licensing stage but limited public disclosure of independent board composition or audit arrangements.

sources.label: CBUAE governance requirements for payment token issuers

technology.label: Technology & Custody
technology.rationale: AE Coin deployed on permissioned and public chain rails (specific chain coverage developing). Smart contract architecture and audit reports not as publicly documented as established peers. Reserve custody at UAE-licensed banks per CBUAE requirements. Technology stack maturity assessed as developing given regime is in early implementation.

redemption.label: Redemption Mechanics
redemption.rationale: CBUAE Payment Token Services Regulation requires par-redemption right. AE Coin issuer commits to 1:1 redemption against AED bank credit. Settlement mechanics rely on UAE domestic banking rails. Public-facing redemption terms documented but operational track record limited given recent licensing. Cross-border redemption pathways less established than USD-pegged peers.

disclosure.label: Public Disclosure
disclosure.rationale: CBUAE supervisory reporting required but public-facing disclosure standards (attestation cadence, reserve composition granularity, independent auditor reports) not yet established at MiCA/MAS levels. Insufficient public data to assess disclosure quality with confidence. Status reflects regime newness and operational ramp rather than identified deficiency.

reserve_details: 100% AED-denominated backing required under CBUAE Payment Token Services Regulation. Reserves held at UAE-licensed banks under segregation requirements. Public composition disclosures less granular than MiCA/MAS issuers; independent attestation framework still developing under new regime.
governance_notes: Independent UAE-incorporated entity under CBUAE supervision. Senior management subject to CBUAE fit-and-proper review. Risk and compliance functions established at licensing stage. Limited public disclosure of board composition and independent oversight arrangements.
technology_notes: Multi-chain deployment with focus on UAE domestic payment infrastructure compatibility. Reserve custody at UAE-licensed banks. Smart contract documentation and third-party audit reports less publicly accessible than established global peers.
redemption_notes: Par-redemption right per CBUAE regulation. Settlement via UAE banking rails. Operational track record limited given recent (2024) licensing. Cross-border redemption infrastructure less developed than USD/EUR-pegged peers.
disclosure_notes: Supervisory reporting to CBUAE required but public disclosure cadence and granularity below MiCA/MAS standards. Regime is in early implementation; market standards expected to mature with broader licensing and operational scale.
reviewer_note: Reference case for UAE's federal payment token regime under CBUAE. Note distinct from VARA virtual asset framework (Dubai) and FSRA framework (ADGM) — UAE regulatory landscape fragments across multiple competent authorities. AE Coin signals reflect regime newness rather than identified structural deficiency; expect signal upgrades as operational track record and disclosure cadence develop.

name: Paxos (USDP)
summary: US-dollar stablecoin issued by Paxos Trust Company under a New York State Department of Financial Services (NYDFS) limited-purpose trust charter. One of the earliest regulated USD stablecoins (launched 2018). NYDFS framework imposes trust-company prudential requirements including 1:1 reserve backing, monthly attestations, and direct state supervision.

capital_adequacy.label: Capital Adequacy
capital_adequacy.rationale: Paxos Trust Company holds NYDFS limited-purpose trust charter subject to NY Banking Law Article III-A requirements, including minimum capital and ongoing prudential standards. NYDFS imposes additional stablecoin-specific guidance (June 2022) on reserve requirements and capital adequacy. Group-level support via Paxos parent entity.

reserve_quality.label: Reserve Quality
reserve_quality.rationale: NYDFS stablecoin guidance requires 1:1 backing in qualifying high-quality liquid assets: US Treasury bills with ≤3-month maturity, overnight reverse repos collateralised by Treasuries, and insured deposits at US banks. Paxos publishes monthly reserve composition reports. Bankruptcy-remote treatment under NY Banking Law trust provisions provides structural protection to token holders.

governance.label: Governance
governance.rationale: Trust company structure imposes fiduciary duties to token holders under NY Banking Law. NYDFS direct supervision with ongoing examination cycle. Senior management subject to NYDFS fit-and-proper review; independent board members; documented risk management and compliance framework. Additional regulatory footprint via MAS (Singapore) and ADGM FSRA (Abu Dhabi).

sources.label: NY Banking Law Article III-A (trust company supervision)

technology.label: Technology & Custody
technology.rationale: USDP deployed on Ethereum with verified, audited smart contracts. Mint/burn controls via Paxos Trust operational keys with multi-sig governance. Reserve custody self-custodied as trust company (Paxos is itself a qualified custodian under NY Banking Law). Smart contract includes freeze and blacklist functions for sanctions compliance.

redemption.label: Redemption Mechanics
redemption.rationale: Paxos provides 1:1 USD redemption to verified Paxos account holders, typically same-day for institutional and T+1 for retail. NYDFS trust framework imposes redemption obligation as fiduciary duty. No redemption fees for standard transactions. Bank-grade settlement infrastructure leverages Paxos's trust company banking relationships.

disclosure.label: Public Disclosure
disclosure.rationale: Monthly third-party attestations by independent registered public accounting firm. Public reserve composition reports with CUSIP-level detail on Treasury holdings. NYDFS receives ongoing supervisory reporting. Paxos publishes annual transparency reports. Disclosure cadence comparable to MiCA-regulated peers.

reserve_details: 1:1 backing in NYDFS-qualifying assets: US Treasury bills with residual maturity ≤3 months, overnight reverse repurchase agreements fully collateralised by Treasury securities, and FDIC-insured deposits at US banks. Assets held in segregated trust accounts under NY Banking Law fiduciary segregation rules — bankruptcy-remote from Paxos Trust general creditors.
governance_notes: NYDFS limited-purpose trust charter subjects Paxos to NY Banking Law Article III-A trust company supervision. Fiduciary duties to token holders embedded in trust structure. Independent board members; risk management, compliance, and internal audit functions with direct reporting lines to senior management and board. Senior management subject to NYDFS fit-and-proper review.
technology_notes: USDP issued natively on Ethereum mainnet (no off-chain ledger backing the on-chain supply). Mint/burn operations controlled by Paxos Trust multi-sig keys. Smart contract supports compliance-driven freeze and blacklist functions enforceable by Paxos against sanctioned addresses. Public verified contract; periodic third-party security audits.
redemption_notes: Direct 1:1 USD redemption available to Paxos account holders with KYC verification. Settlement via Paxos trust company banking relationships — typically same-day for institutional clients, T+1 for retail. Secondary market redemption via exchanges. No redemption fees on standard transactions.
disclosure_notes: Monthly attestations by independent auditor with reserve composition published at CUSIP level. NYDFS receives ongoing supervisory and prudential reporting. Annual transparency reports. Disclosure standards established prior to MiCA framework and broadly comparable.
reviewer_note: Reference case for US state-trust model (NYDFS limited-purpose trust charter). Structurally analogous to Japan's trust-type stablecoin model under revised PSA. Federal stablecoin framework (GENIUS Act / STABLE Act) under congressional consideration would supersede state-only regimes if enacted; Paxos positioned to transition under either path.

name: PayPal USD (PYUSD)
summary: US-dollar stablecoin issued by Paxos Trust Company under NYDFS limited-purpose trust charter, branded and distributed by PayPal Holdings. Platform-issuer separation structure: regulatory and reserve responsibility sits with Paxos; consumer-facing wallet, distribution, and brand sit with PayPal. First major US payments platform to launch a regulated stablecoin (2023).

capital_adequacy.label: Capital Adequacy
capital_adequacy.rationale: Issuance entity Paxos Trust Company subject to NYDFS limited-purpose trust charter prudential requirements under NY Banking Law Article III-A. Distinct from PayPal Holdings (the brand partner) which itself is regulated as a money transmitter across US states but does not hold reserve obligations. Capital adequacy inherits from Paxos Trust framework, well-established since 2018.

reserve_quality.label: Reserve Quality
reserve_quality.rationale: Reserves managed by Paxos Trust under NYDFS guidance: US Treasury bills (≤3 months), overnight reverse repos collateralised by Treasuries, and insured USD deposits at US banks. Bankruptcy-remote trust segregation under NY Banking Law. Monthly third-party attestations by WithumSmith+Brown PC published on Paxos transparency portal.

governance.label: Governance
governance.rationale: Paxos Trust governance well-established and NYDFS-supervised (positive). However, platform-issuer separation creates governance complexity: consumer-facing risk management, communications, and incident response are bifurcated between Paxos (regulated entity) and PayPal (brand partner). Public clarity on responsibility allocation during stress scenarios less developed than vertically integrated issuers (Circle, Tether).

technology.label: Technology & Custody
technology.rationale: PYUSD deployed on Ethereum and Solana with audited smart contracts. Mint/burn controlled by Paxos Trust multi-sig operational keys. Reserve custody self-custodied by Paxos as NYDFS-qualified custodian. Smart contracts include sanctions-compliance freeze and blacklist functions.

redemption.label: Redemption Mechanics
redemption.rationale: Direct 1:1 redemption available to Paxos account holders with KYC verification (T+1 typical). PayPal-side redemption pathway differs: PayPal users redeem PYUSD via in-app conversion to USD account balance, with conversion settled via Paxos. Two-tier redemption introduces operational dependency on PayPal availability for retail users — a structural distinction from direct-issuer redemption models.

disclosure.label: Public Disclosure
disclosure.rationale: Monthly attestations by WithumSmith+Brown PC published on Paxos transparency portal with reserve composition at CUSIP level. NYDFS ongoing supervisory reporting. PayPal Holdings public-company disclosures (10-K, 10-Q) provide additional brand-partner transparency. Disclosure cadence comparable to direct-issuer stablecoins.

reserve_details: 1:1 backing in NYDFS-qualifying assets managed by Paxos Trust: short-dated US Treasury bills, overnight reverse repos collateralised by Treasuries, and FDIC-insured USD deposits. Assets held in segregated trust accounts under NY Banking Law fiduciary segregation — bankruptcy-remote from both Paxos Trust general creditors and PayPal Holdings.
governance_notes: Platform-issuer separation: Paxos Trust Company is the regulated issuer and reserve holder; PayPal Holdings is the brand partner and distribution channel. Paxos governance under NYDFS limited-purpose trust framework; PayPal under various US state money transmitter licences. Coordination during stress scenarios depends on bilateral contract terms not publicly disclosed in full detail.
technology_notes: PYUSD deployed natively on Ethereum and Solana. ERC-20 standard on Ethereum with sanctions-compliance freeze functions. Mint/burn controlled by Paxos Trust multi-sig keys; PayPal does not hold mint authority. Reserve custody self-managed by Paxos as NYDFS-qualified custodian.
redemption_notes: Bifurcated redemption pathways: institutional Paxos account holders redeem directly at 1:1 (T+1 typical). PayPal app users convert PYUSD to USD balance within PayPal infrastructure, with settlement between PayPal and Paxos. Retail users rely on PayPal availability and terms; operational dependency is a structural feature of platform-issuer separation.
disclosure_notes: Monthly attestations on Paxos transparency portal with CUSIP-level Treasury detail. NYDFS ongoing prudential reporting. PayPal Holdings 10-K/10-Q SEC filings provide additional brand-partner financial transparency. Disclosure framework inherits from Paxos USDP infrastructure.
reviewer_note: Reference case for platform-issuer separation architecture in US stablecoin market — distinct structural archetype from vertically integrated issuers (Circle, Tether) and pure regulated trust issuers (Paxos USDP). Governance and redemption signals reflect operational complexity of bifurcated responsibility model, not deficiency in either entity.

name: Circle (USDC)
summary: US-dollar stablecoin issued by Circle Internet Financial. Operates under a patchwork of US state money transmitter licences (MTLs) across ~46 states, NYDFS BitLicense, and federal AML/CFT registrations. Largest fully-reserved USD stablecoin by market capitalisation. Distinct legal entity from Circle Mint France SAS (EURC issuer).

capital_adequacy.label: Capital Adequacy
capital_adequacy.rationale: Operates under state MTL framework which imposes net-worth and surety bond requirements but is materially less stringent than trust company charter (Paxos) or MiCA EMT regime (EURC). NYDFS BitLicense imposes additional capital floor. Federal stablecoin framework (GENIUS Act / STABLE Act) pending. Circle Internet Financial's SEC filings disclose group capital position. State-by-state patchwork creates supervisory gaps relative to single-licence regimes.

reserve_quality.label: Reserve Quality
reserve_quality.rationale: Reserves held in segregated accounts at US banks plus Circle Reserve Fund — a SEC-registered government MMF (ticker USDXX) managed by BlackRock investing in short-dated US Treasuries, repos collateralised by Treasuries, and cash. Post-SVB (March 2023) reserve concentration was reduced; current structure diversified across multiple custodian banks. Monthly attestations by Deloitte.

governance.label: Governance
governance.rationale: Multi-jurisdictional regulatory oversight: NYDFS BitLicense, ~46 state MTLs, FCA EMI registration (UK), ACPR EMI licence (France via Circle Mint France subsidiary). Public company structure post-SEC filings provides board independence, audit committee, and disclosure obligations. Senior management subject to NYDFS and state regulator fit-and-proper assessments.

technology.label: Technology & Custody
technology.rationale: USDC deployed natively on 15+ blockchains including Ethereum, Solana, Avalanche, Base, Arbitrum, Polygon, with native cross-chain transfer protocol (CCTP) replacing legacy bridges. Audited smart contracts (ChainSecurity, Halborn). Reserve custody at regulated US banks and SEC-registered MMF. Role-based controls for mint/burn/blocklist functions; HSM-based key management.

redemption.label: Redemption Mechanics
redemption.rationale: Direct 1:1 USD redemption available to Circle Mint customers (KYC-verified institutions) with T+1 settlement and no fees. Retail holders redeem via exchanges and licensed partners. Post-SVB stress test (March 2023): USDC briefly traded below peg for ~3 days while redemption was paused due to Federal Reserve banking holiday weekend; full redemption restored on T+3. Demonstrated resilience but also revealed off-hour banking dependency.

disclosure.label: Public Disclosure
disclosure.rationale: Monthly third-party attestations by Deloitte covering reserve composition and value. Weekly publication of reserve composition with CUSIP-level Treasury holdings. NYDFS receives ongoing supervisory reporting. SEC public-company filings provide audited annual financials. Disclosure cadence sets industry benchmark; post-SVB incident disclosures were timely and substantive.

reserve_details: Reserves diversified across multiple US-regulated bank deposits and the Circle Reserve Fund (SEC-registered government MMF managed by BlackRock investing in short-dated Treasuries, Treasury repos, and cash). Post-March 2023 SVB incident, custodian concentration reduced; current structure spreads bank deposits across multiple G-SIB and large US banks. Bankruptcy-remote treatment depends on state-by-state MTL framework rather than single uniform trust law — a structural difference from NYDFS trust issuers (Paxos USDP).
governance_notes: Public-company governance via SEC filings — independent board, audit committee, disclosure controls. Multi-jurisdiction regulatory footprint: NYDFS, ~46 state MTLs, FCA UK EMI registration, French ACPR EMI licence (Circle Mint France SAS for EURC). Cross-border group structure adds complexity but also provides diversified supervisory coverage.
technology_notes: Native multi-chain deployment via Circle's Cross-Chain Transfer Protocol (CCTP) — replaces legacy bridge architecture with burn-and-mint mechanism. 15+ supported chains. Audited smart contracts with role-based access controls. Sanctions-compliance freeze and blacklist functions enforceable globally.
redemption_notes: Two-tier redemption: institutional Circle Mint clients redeem directly 1:1 with T+1 settlement; retail via exchanges and licensed partners. March 2023 SVB event demonstrated both resilience (full redemption restored within 3 days) and structural dependency on US banking hours — a known limitation of bank-deposit-backed stablecoins relative to MMF-only or central-bank-reserve models.
disclosure_notes: Industry-benchmark disclosure cadence: monthly Deloitte attestations, weekly reserve composition with CUSIP-level Treasury detail, real-time on-chain circulation. SEC public-company disclosures add layer of audited annual transparency. Post-SVB incident communication was timely and substantive — a positive stress-test of the disclosure regime.

citations.label: GENIUS Act / STABLE Act federal stablecoin frameworks (pending)

reviewer_note: Largest USD stablecoin and benchmark for multi-jurisdiction operating model. Note structural distinction between Circle's US entity (state MTL + NYDFS BitLicense framework) and EU entity (Circle Mint France SAS under MiCA EMT) — same group, two regulatory wrappers, materially different prudential floors. SVB March 2023 event is the most studied stress test of a major regulated stablecoin and is documented in the Lessons Learned section.

name: Paxos Digital (USDG)
parent: Paxos Digital Singapore Pte. Ltd. (Paxos Group)
summary: US-dollar stablecoin issued by Paxos Digital Singapore under MAS Major Payment Institution licence with Single-Currency Stablecoin (SCS) issuance scope. Designed for compliance with the Global Dollar Network — a consortium model that shares stablecoin economics with distribution partners. Distinct from Paxos USDP (US/NYDFS) despite shared parent group.

capital_adequacy.label: Capital Adequacy
capital_adequacy.rationale: MAS SCS Framework requires base capital of SGD 1 million or 50% of annual operating expenses (whichever higher). Paxos Digital Singapore operates under Major Payment Institution licence with explicit SCS scope. Group-level support from Paxos Trust Company (US NYDFS-regulated parent affiliate) provides additional structural backing.

reserve_quality.label: Reserve Quality
reserve_quality.rationale: Reserves held in segregated accounts at MAS-licensed banks. MAS SCS Framework permits cash, cash equivalents, and short-term debt securities (≤3-month residual maturity) issued by central banks or sovereigns with credit rating ≥AA-. Monthly attestations by independent auditor; reserve composition publicly disclosed via Paxos transparency framework.

governance.label: Governance
governance.rationale: MPI licence requires MAS fit-and-proper assessment of CEO, directors, substantial shareholders. Singapore-incorporated entity with MAS-supervised local management. Group-level oversight via Paxos Group; cross-jurisdictional regulatory footprint (NYDFS US, FSRA Abu Dhabi). Risk management and compliance functions documented at licensing stage.

sources.label: MAS Payment Services Act licensee obligations

technology.label: Technology & Custody
technology.rationale: USDG deployed on Ethereum and Solana with audited smart contracts. Mint/burn controlled by Paxos operational keys with multi-sig governance. Reserve custody at MAS-licensed banks. Smart contracts include compliance-driven freeze and blacklist functions. Inherits Paxos Group security architecture established for USDP and PYUSD.

redemption.label: Redemption Mechanics
redemption.rationale: MAS SCS Framework mandates par redemption within 5 business days of request. Paxos Digital Singapore customers redeem 1:1 against USD bank deposits. Institutional customers via direct Paxos integration; retail via partner exchanges in the Global Dollar Network consortium. No redemption fees per MAS SCS rules.

disclosure.label: Public Disclosure
disclosure.rationale: Monthly third-party attestations published on Paxos transparency portal with reserve composition. MAS-regulated label restricted to compliant issuers; USDG displays MAS-regulated marker. MAS receives ongoing prudential reporting. Whitepaper filed with MAS. Disclosure cadence consistent with Paxos Group standards established for USDP/PYUSD.

reserve_details: Reserves held in segregated accounts at MAS-licensed banks per SCS Framework. Composition limited to USD cash, cash equivalents, and short-dated debt securities (≤3-month residual maturity) from qualifying sovereign or central bank issuers with ≥AA- credit rating. Bankruptcy-remote treatment under Singapore trust law applicable to segregated payment account assets.
governance_notes: Singapore-incorporated subsidiary under Paxos Group. CEO, directors, and substantial shareholders subject to MAS fit-and-proper review. Risk management and compliance functions established at licensing; group-level oversight via Paxos US affiliate provides additional governance coordination. Substantial shareholders disclosed to MAS per PS Act.
technology_notes: USDG deployed on Ethereum and Solana; expansion to additional chains planned. Mint/burn controlled by Paxos multi-sig keys with operational segregation. Smart contracts include freeze and blacklist functions for compliance enforcement. Security architecture inherits from Paxos Group infrastructure validated through USDP and PYUSD operations.
redemption_notes: Two-tier redemption: institutional Paxos Digital clients redeem directly with T+1 typical settlement; retail users access redemption via Global Dollar Network consortium exchange partners. MAS SCS rules cap redemption settlement at 5 business days with no fees permitted.
disclosure_notes: Monthly attestations on Paxos transparency portal — same framework as USDP and PYUSD. MAS-regulated marker visible on official USDG channels. MAS receives ongoing prudential and operational reporting. Public real-time circulation observable on supported blockchains.
reviewer_note: Second major MAS SCS-licensed issuer. Reference case for consortium-economics model: Global Dollar Network shares stablecoin yield with distribution partners (exchanges, wallets) — a structurally distinct business model from direct-issuer-monopoly stablecoins (USDC, USDT). MAS SCS scope at the issuer level is functionally similar to MiCA EMT; key differences are redemption window (5 business days vs MiCA's at-any-time) and reserve eligibility criteria.

name: Tether (USDT)
parent: Tether Holdings Limited (BVI) / Tether International Limited (El Salvador)
summary: US-dollar stablecoin issued by Tether group, the largest stablecoin by market capitalisation globally. Operates without licensing under MiCA, MAS SCS, or NYDFS frameworks. Re-domiciled key operations to El Salvador (Digital Assets framework) in 2025. Not authorised for distribution to EEA users under MiCA from 2025; remains widely used in offshore and emerging markets. Included as reference case for unregulated-incumbent structural archetype.

capital_adequacy.label: Capital Adequacy
capital_adequacy.rationale: Tether discloses substantial excess reserves and group equity via quarterly attestations, but operates outside any major prudential supervisor with binding capital requirements. El Salvador Digital Assets framework imposes lighter prudential floor than MiCA, MAS SCS, or NYDFS trust regimes. Absence of independent supervisory enforcement makes capital adequacy assessment dependent on issuer self-disclosure rather than verifiable regulatory floors.

sources.label: El Salvador Digital Assets Issuance Law

reserve_quality.label: Reserve Quality
reserve_quality.rationale: Recent attestations report majority US Treasury bill holdings plus cash, repos, and money market funds — a material improvement over the commercial-paper-heavy composition disclosed in 2021. However, attestations are not full audits, are produced by smaller accounting firms relative to peers (BDO replacing prior firms), and historical reserve-quality disclosures included assets (commercial paper, secured loans, precious metals, other investments) that would not qualify under MiCA/MAS/NYDFS reserve eligibility rules. Disclosure granularity below MiCA-regulated peers.

governance.label: Governance
governance.rationale: No supervisory authority with binding fit-and-proper assessment of senior management. NYDFS and CFTC enforcement actions (2021) cited material misrepresentations regarding USDT backing; settled without admission of liability. Group structure spans BVI, El Salvador, and various operating affiliates with limited public disclosure of consolidated governance framework. Independent board composition not publicly documented at standards comparable to MiCA/MAS/NYDFS issuers.

technology.label: Technology & Custody
technology.rationale: USDT deployed on 14+ blockchains. Smart contracts include freeze and blacklist functions used cooperatively with law enforcement requests. Reserve custody arrangements disclosed in attestations but third-party audit reports on smart contract security less publicly accessible than peers. Multi-chain deployment includes legacy chains (Omni, Liquid) with operational complexity.

redemption.label: Redemption Mechanics
redemption.rationale: Direct redemption available only to verified Tether clients with minimum USD 100,000 transaction sizes and 0.1% fee — materially more restrictive than MiCA/MAS/NYDFS issuers which mandate at-par redemption without fees and without minimum thresholds. Retail holders depend on secondary market liquidity. Past periods (May 2022 stress event) saw USDT briefly trade below peg before recovering; redemption infrastructure absorbed the stress but the redemption-friction model is structurally distinct from regulated peers.

disclosure.label: Public Disclosure
disclosure.rationale: Quarterly attestations by BDO Italia covering reserve composition at category level (not CUSIP-level). No full annual audit despite repeated public commitments. Disclosure cadence and granularity materially below MiCA/MAS/NYDFS-regulated peers. Group financial statements not subject to public-company SEC disclosure regime. Self-published transparency reports useful directionally but insufficient for full structural verification.

reserve_details: Recent attestations report majority allocation to US Treasury bills (held directly and via repo arrangements) plus cash, MMFs, secured loans, precious metals (gold), Bitcoin, and other investments. Composition includes assets (gold, BTC, secured loans, other investments) that would not qualify under MiCA, MAS SCS, or NYDFS reserve eligibility rules. Reserve segregation arrangements disclosed at high level but not subject to enforceable supervisory segregation requirements equivalent to MiCA Article 36-38 or NYDFS guidance.
governance_notes: Multi-entity group structure (Tether Holdings BVI, Tether International El Salvador, Tether Operations Limited, various operating affiliates) with limited public consolidated governance disclosure. CEO and senior management identifiable but not subject to binding fit-and-proper assessment by major prudential supervisor. NYDFS and CFTC 2021 enforcement actions resulted in monetary settlements and ongoing reserve attestation obligations under NY Attorney General agreement (terminated 2023).
technology_notes: Multi-chain deployment across Ethereum, Tron, Solana, Avalanche, Algorand, Liquid, Omni, and others. Tron currently hosts majority of circulating supply. Smart contract freeze and blacklist functions used cooperatively with law enforcement requests (US DOJ, OFAC). Public smart contract code; third-party security audit reports less prominently published than peers.
redemption_notes: Friction-based redemption model: USD 100,000 minimum transaction, 0.1% fee, verified-client-only access. Retail holders rely on secondary market liquidity rather than direct redemption — a structural distinction from at-par-no-fee redemption mandated under MiCA, MAS SCS, and NYDFS frameworks. May 2022 stress event saw temporary depeg; redemption infrastructure absorbed the stress within days.
disclosure_notes: Quarterly attestations (not audits) by BDO Italia covering reserve composition at category level. No full annual audit by Big Four accounting firm despite repeated public commitments since 2017. NY Attorney General settlement (2021) required quarterly attestations through 2023 (now expired). Self-published reports useful directionally but insufficient for structural verification at MiCA/MAS/NYDFS standards.

citations.label: El Salvador Digital Assets Issuance Law

reviewer_note: Reference case for unregulated-incumbent structural archetype. Tether's market dominance (largest stablecoin by circulation) coexists with the weakest supervisory coverage among major USD stablecoins. Critical comparator for analyzing the policy trade-off between regulated market shares (Circle, Paxos) and unregulated liquidity (Tether) — a recurring theme in stablecoin policy discourse. Status marked 'under_review' rather than 'rejected' or 'licensed' reflects ongoing regulatory ambiguity: not authorised under MiCA, not licensed by MAS or NYDFS, but not formally prohibited globally.

name: Gemini Dollar (GUSD)
summary: US-dollar stablecoin issued by Gemini Trust Company under New York State Department of Financial Services limited-purpose trust charter. Approved alongside Paxos USDP in September 2018 as one of the first two NYDFS-regulated USD stablecoins. Operated by Gemini exchange's regulated trust company subsidiary; smaller circulation than Paxos USDP but identical regulatory framework.

capital_adequacy.label: Capital Adequacy
capital_adequacy.rationale: Gemini Trust Company holds NYDFS limited-purpose trust charter subject to NY Banking Law Article III-A capital and prudential requirements. NYDFS June 2022 stablecoin guidance applies. Trust company status imposes ongoing capital adequacy assessment by NYDFS examiners. Group-level support from Gemini exchange parent, though parent has reduced workforce significantly during 2022-2024 crypto market contraction.

reserve_quality.label: Reserve Quality
reserve_quality.rationale: Reserves held in segregated accounts at US banks per NYDFS guidance: US Treasury bills (≤3-month maturity), overnight reverse repos collateralised by Treasuries, and insured deposits at US banks. Monthly third-party attestations by BPM LLP. Bankruptcy-remote treatment under NY Banking Law trust segregation provisions.

governance.label: Governance
governance.rationale: Trust company structure imposes fiduciary duties to token holders under NY Banking Law. NYDFS direct supervision with ongoing examination cycle. However, parent Gemini exchange faced enforcement actions and customer fund disputes via Genesis lending product (Earn programme) — separate from GUSD reserves but raising group-level governance concerns. Senior management subject to NYDFS fit-and-proper review at trust company level.

technology.label: Technology & Custody
technology.rationale: GUSD deployed on Ethereum with verified, audited smart contracts. ERC-20 standard with mint/burn controls under Gemini Trust operational keys. Smart contract supports freeze and blacklist functions for sanctions compliance. Reserve custody at US banks per NYDFS qualifying custodian requirements.

redemption.label: Redemption Mechanics
redemption.rationale: Gemini provides 1:1 USD redemption to verified Gemini account holders, typically same-day for institutional and T+1 for retail. NYDFS trust framework imposes redemption obligation as fiduciary duty. Banking-rail settlement via Gemini Trust banking relationships. Same structural framework as Paxos USDP.

disclosure.label: Public Disclosure
disclosure.rationale: Monthly third-party attestations by BPM LLP covering reserve composition and value. Reserve composition published on Gemini Dollar transparency page. NYDFS receives ongoing supervisory reporting. Disclosure cadence comparable to Paxos USDP (the closest peer under identical regulatory framework).

reserve_details: 1:1 backing in NYDFS-qualifying assets: US Treasury bills with residual maturity ≤3 months, overnight reverse repos collateralised by Treasuries, and FDIC-insured deposits at US banks. Held in segregated trust accounts under NY Banking Law fiduciary segregation — bankruptcy-remote from Gemini Trust general creditors. Reserve structure identical in framework to Paxos USDP.
governance_notes: NYDFS limited-purpose trust charter subjects Gemini Trust Company to NY Banking Law Article III-A trust company supervision. Fiduciary duties to token holders embedded in trust structure. Senior management subject to NYDFS fit-and-proper assessment. Group-level governance concerns: parent Gemini's Earn programme dispute (resolved February 2024 via NYDFS settlement) sits at exchange-affiliate level, not GUSD reserves — but informs broader assessment of parent risk culture.
technology_notes: GUSD issued natively on Ethereum mainnet. Mint/burn operations controlled by Gemini Trust multi-sig keys. Smart contract supports compliance-driven freeze and blacklist functions. Public verified contract; smaller multi-chain footprint than peers (Ethereum-only) reflects design choice and smaller circulation.
redemption_notes: Direct 1:1 USD redemption to Gemini account holders. Settlement via Gemini Trust banking relationships. Institutional clients typically same-day; retail T+1. Secondary market redemption via exchanges with smaller liquidity depth than larger peer stablecoins (USDC, USDP).
disclosure_notes: Monthly attestations by BPM LLP with reserve composition disclosure. NYDFS receives ongoing supervisory and prudential reporting. Disclosure framework structurally identical to Paxos USDP; both established under September 2018 NYDFS approval as templates for the regulated USD stablecoin model.
reviewer_note: Co-pioneer of NYDFS-regulated USD stablecoin model alongside Paxos USDP (both approved September 2018). Useful direct comparator under identical regulatory framework: GUSD-USDP signal differences isolate issuer-specific factors (governance, scale) from regime-level factors. Smaller circulation than USDP reflects market dynamics rather than supervisory differences.

name: Banking Circle (EURI)
summary: Euro-denominated e-money token issued by Banking Circle Group under MiCA EMT regime via Luxembourg authorisation. Banking Circle operates as a fully licensed bank in Luxembourg (CSSF-supervised), making EURI a bank-issued EMT structurally similar to SG-Forge EURCV. Targets payments and settlement use cases within Banking Circle's institutional client base.

capital_adequacy.label: Capital Adequacy
capital_adequacy.rationale: Banking Circle Group operates under Luxembourg credit institution licence (CSSF-authorised), subject to CRR/CRD IV capital adequacy requirements far exceeding EMI thresholds. MiCA Article 35 own funds requirement is comfortably met within bank prudential framework. Direct ECB supervision via SSM applies to significant credit institutions in the eurozone.

reserve_quality.label: Reserve Quality
reserve_quality.rationale: Reserves held on Banking Circle's balance sheet under credit institution status — providing reserve quality structurally stronger than non-bank EMT issuers since deposits are protected by CRR liquidity coverage and net stable funding requirements. MiCA segregation rules ensure bankruptcy-remote treatment of token-holder claims under Luxembourg law.

governance.label: Governance
governance.rationale: Banking Circle governance under CSSF supervision per Luxembourg banking law. Senior management subject to fit-and-proper review. Documented risk management, compliance, and internal audit functions per CRD IV requirements. Owned by EQT (private equity) with institutional governance practices. Banking-grade governance standards apply.

technology.label: Technology & Custody
technology.rationale: EURI deployed on Ethereum and Solana with audited smart contracts. Mint/burn controlled by Banking Circle operational keys under banking-grade key management infrastructure. Reserve custody on bank balance sheet — leveraging existing banking custody framework. Smart contracts include compliance-driven freeze functions.

redemption.label: Redemption Mechanics
redemption.rationale: MiCA Article 39 par-redemption right applies. Banking Circle customers redeem 1:1 against EUR bank account credits via existing banking payment rails. Bank-issuer model provides natural redemption infrastructure leveraging Banking Circle's institutional banking client base. No redemption fees per MiCA prohibition.

disclosure.label: Public Disclosure
disclosure.rationale: Group-level Pillar 3 disclosures under CRR apply to Banking Circle as Luxembourg credit institution. Whitepaper notified to CSSF per MiCA Article 51. Annual audited financial statements published. EURI on-chain circulation observable via public blockchain. CSSF receives ongoing supervisory reporting.

reserve_details: Reserves backed by deposits on Banking Circle credit institution balance sheet. Bank-issuer model differs structurally from non-bank EMI issuers (Circle): token-holder claims rank as e-money obligations with MiCA segregation protections; in resolution scenarios, BRRD (Bank Recovery and Resolution Directive) applies in parallel. CRR liquidity coverage and net stable funding requirements provide structural reserve quality assurance beyond MiCA minimums.
governance_notes: Wholly-owned subsidiary structure under EQT (private equity ownership). Luxembourg-incorporated bank with CSSF as home-state supervisor. Board composition includes group-appointed and independent directors per CRD IV. Risk and compliance functions established under banking framework; MiCA-specific compliance layered on top.
technology_notes: EURI issued natively on Ethereum and Solana. Mint/burn controlled by Banking Circle multi-sig keys with banking-grade HSM protection. Smart contracts support compliance freeze functions. Public verified contracts; multi-chain deployment supports Banking Circle's payment rail integration use case.
redemption_notes: Bank-issuer redemption advantage: holders redeem via standard EUR bank credit through Banking Circle's institutional banking infrastructure. Targeted at institutional clients; retail redemption through partner exchanges. MiCA prohibits redemption fees; Banking Circle's existing payment rail relationships provide efficient settlement.
disclosure_notes: Group-level CRR Pillar 3 disclosures provide structural transparency. Whitepaper filed with CSSF per MiCA Article 51. Annual audited group financials. EURI on-chain circulation observable via Ethereum and Solana block explorers. CSSF receives ongoing supervisory reporting under dual banking + MiCA framework.
reviewer_note: Second bank-issued EMT under MiCA (alongside SG-Forge EURCV). Reference case for Luxembourg authorisation path. CSSF-supervised credit institution issuance route under MiCA Title III provides structurally robust EMT issuance template. Banking Circle's institutional payment client base distinguishes use case from retail-oriented EMT issuers (Circle EURC).

name: OSL Stablecoin (HK)
summary: HKD-pegged stablecoin proposal by OSL Group, a Hong Kong-listed digital asset platform operator. Widely tipped as a first-batch contender given OSL's SFC Type 1 and Type 7 licences for virtual asset trading. Not selected in the first batch of HKMA stablecoin issuer licences announced 10 April 2026; OSL acknowledged the outcome publicly and indicated continued engagement with the regulatory framework.

capital_adequacy.label: Capital Adequacy
capital_adequacy.rationale: OSL Group is HKEX-listed (stock code 863) with public-company capital disclosures via interim and annual reports. However, group-level capital position has been volatile relative to bank-issuer competitors (HSBC, Standard Chartered consortium) selected in the first batch. HKMA application materials not public; rejection rationale not officially disclosed but media reporting cited preference for issuers with deep traditional finance and risk management track records.

reserve_quality.label: Reserve Quality
reserve_quality.rationale: Reserve composition design not publicly disclosed at application stage. Stablecoins Ordinance Cap. 649 requires 1:1 backing in high-quality liquid assets held at HKMA-authorised institutions. Without public attestation framework or operational track record, reserve quality assessment is not feasible from available information.

governance.label: Governance
governance.rationale: HKEX-listed public company governance applies — independent non-executive directors, audit committee, listing-rule disclosures. SFC Type 1 and Type 7 licences impose conduct and capital requirements. However, HKMA's first-batch selection criteria reportedly prioritised traditional banking risk management depth; OSL's profile as a digital-asset-native operator was disadvantaged relative to HSBC and Standard Chartered consortium. Governance gap is regulatory-preference-driven rather than structural deficiency.

sources.label: HKMA deputy CE Darryl Chan remarks on selection criteria

technology.label: Technology & Custody
technology.rationale: OSL operates regulated digital asset trading and custody infrastructure under SFC oversight, providing technological capability foundation. Specific stablecoin smart contract architecture, custody arrangements, and audit reports not public. Stablecoin issuance would have leveraged existing regulated custody framework but operational track record for stablecoin-specific infrastructure not yet established.

redemption.label: Redemption Mechanics
redemption.rationale: Redemption design not publicly disclosed at application stage. Stablecoins Ordinance requires par-redemption framework. Without authorised issuance, redemption mechanics remain hypothetical; no operational track record available for assessment.

disclosure.label: Public Disclosure
disclosure.rationale: HKEX-listed public-company disclosure regime applies at parent level — interim and annual reports, continuing disclosure obligations under Listing Rules. SFC supervision adds further disclosure cadence. Stablecoin-specific transparency framework not yet operationalised given non-licensure. Public-company disclosure baseline exists but stablecoin-specific reserve attestation framework not yet implemented.

reserve_details: Reserve composition design not publicly disclosed at application stage. Under Stablecoins Ordinance Cap. 649, licensed issuers would be required to maintain 1:1 backing in high-quality liquid assets at HKMA-authorised institutions with bankruptcy-remote segregation. OSL's non-selection in first batch means no operational reserve arrangement exists for SARM evaluation.
governance_notes: HKEX-listed (stock code 863) with public-company governance: independent directors, audit committee, Listing Rules disclosures. SFC Type 1 (dealing in securities) and Type 7 (providing automated trading services) regulated activities. HKMA first-batch selection reportedly prioritised traditional bank risk management depth over digital-asset-native operator profiles — a regulatory preference signalling about the HK regime's structural direction.
technology_notes: OSL operates SFC-regulated digital asset trading and custody infrastructure with operational track record. Stablecoin-specific smart contract architecture and custody design not implemented given non-licensure. Existing digital asset custody framework would have provided foundation for stablecoin operations.
redemption_notes: Hypothetical only — non-licensure means no operational redemption arrangement exists. Stablecoins Ordinance par-redemption requirement would have applied if licensed.
disclosure_notes: Parent-level HKEX listed disclosure regime provides baseline transparency: interim and annual reports, continuing disclosure obligations under Listing Rules, SFC supervision. Stablecoin-specific transparency framework not operationalised given non-licensure.

citations.label: OSL Group public response to HKMA decision

reviewer_note: First documented HKMA stablecoin licence rejection. Useful as reference case for analysing HKMA's first-batch selection criteria. Reportedly 36 applications were received; only 2 licences granted (HSBC, Anchorpoint Financial). OSL's non-selection signals HKMA preference for traditional bank issuers over digital-asset-native operators in the inaugural batch. Status may evolve if OSL re-applies in subsequent rounds or if regulatory framework expands eligible issuer categories. Multiple SARM dimensions marked 'gray' (insufficient data) reflect non-licensure rather than deficient assessment — operational arrangements simply do not exist for review.

name: GMO Aozora JPY Coin
summary: Japanese-yen stablecoin issued by GMO Aozora Net Bank under the revised Payment Services Act bank-issuance route, deployed via Progmat Coin platform. First major bank-issued JPY stablecoin operationalised under the 2023 PSA revisions. Distinct from Progmat platform itself: GMO Aozora is the licensed issuer; Progmat provides the issuance infrastructure and trust-bank coordination.

capital_adequacy.label: Capital Adequacy
capital_adequacy.rationale: GMO Aozora Net Bank operates under FSA banking licence subject to full banking capital adequacy requirements (Basel III implementation in Japan). Stablecoin issuance under revised PSA does not relax bank prudential floors. Group-level support from GMO Internet Group and Aozora Bank parent (one of Japan's regional banks).

reserve_quality.label: Reserve Quality
reserve_quality.rationale: 100% backing in JPY deposits segregated in trust accounts at MUFG Trust under Progmat Coin platform. Revised PSA requires full asset preservation in trust structure.
