Bankruptcy-remote treatment under Japanese Trust Act — token holders hold beneficiary rights over reserve assets. Structural reserve quality stronger than prepaid-type issuers (JPYC) under same jurisdiction.

governance.label: Governance
governance.rationale: GMO Aozora Net Bank governance under FSA banking supervision per Banking Act. Senior management subject to FSA fit-and-proper review. Independent board members; risk management and internal audit functions per banking framework. Multi-party operational governance: GMO Aozora as issuer, Progmat as platform operator, MUFG Trust as reserve custodian.

technology.label: Technology & Custody
technology.rationale: Deployed via Progmat Coin platform supporting multiple blockchains (Polygon, Avalanche, Ethereum). Permissioned-access architecture with KYC-integrated transfer controls per Japanese regulatory requirements. Reserve custody at MUFG Trust under Japanese trust law. Banking-grade key management for mint/burn operations.

redemption.label: Redemption Mechanics
redemption.rationale: Trust-beneficiary par-redemption right under Japanese trust law. Holders redeem 1:1 against JPY bank deposits at GMO Aozora Net Bank. Banking-rail settlement infrastructure provides reliable redemption pathway. Same-day or T+1 typical for institutional clients via Progmat platform integration.

disclosure.label: Public Disclosure
disclosure.rationale: Bank-level disclosures under FSA banking framework provide structural transparency. Trust-account reserve verification robust at trust bank level. However, stablecoin-specific public attestation cadence less established than MiCA/MAS/NYDFS regimes; per-token transparency portal more limited than international peers. Operational track record short given recent launch.

reserve_details: 100% backing in JPY deposits at GMO Aozora Net Bank, segregated into trust accounts at MUFG Trust Bank under Japanese trust law. Trust beneficiary rights provide bankruptcy-remote treatment for token holders. Structural advantage over prepaid-type issuers (JPYC) which lack trust segregation.
governance_notes: GMO Aozora Net Bank — Japanese commercial bank under FSA Banking Act supervision. Multi-party operational governance with Progmat (platform) and MUFG Trust (reserve custodian). Senior management subject to FSA fit-and-proper assessment. Banking risk management framework applies; MiCA-style stablecoin-specific disclosure layer still developing.
technology_notes: Issuance via Progmat Coin platform with multi-chain support (Polygon, Avalanche, Ethereum). Permissioned-access architecture with KYC integration enforces Japanese regulatory transfer restrictions. Reserve custody under banking infrastructure standards at MUFG Trust.
redemption_notes: Trust-beneficiary par-redemption via GMO Aozora Net Bank rails. Settlement leverages bank's existing payment infrastructure. Institutional clients via Progmat integration; retail access channels expanding as platform matures.
disclosure_notes: Bank-level disclosure framework under FSA Banking Act provides quarterly and annual financial reporting. Trust-account reserve verification structurally robust. Public stablecoin-specific transparency portal less developed than international peers; expected to mature with operational scale.

citations.label: GMO Aozora JPY stablecoin launch announcement

reviewer_note: First operationalised bank-issued JPY stablecoin under revised PSA — distinct from Progmat (platform-only) entry. Demonstrates the bank/trust issuance route in practice. Useful comparator with JPYC (prepaid-type, same jurisdiction) for analysing structural reserve quality and redemption-right differences within a single regulatory environment.

name: Quantoz (EURQ)
summary: Euro-denominated e-money token issued by Quantoz Payments B.V. under Dutch Central Bank (DNB) Electronic Money Institution licence, passporting across the EEA under MiCA EMT regime. Quantoz also issues USDQ (USD-pegged EMT). Designed for compliance-first integration with major exchanges; distributed through partnerships including Kraken and Bitfinex.

capital_adequacy.label: Capital Adequacy
capital_adequacy.rationale: Quantoz Payments B.V. holds Dutch EMI licence under DNB supervision, subject to E-Money Directive 2009/110/EC own funds requirements (minimum EUR 350,000 plus ongoing capital proportional to outstanding e-money). MiCA Article 35 additional 2% requirement for significant issuers applies if thresholds met. Independent EMI structure without bank parent — capital adequacy assessment relies on DNB ongoing supervision.

reserve_quality.label: Reserve Quality
reserve_quality.rationale: Reserves held in segregated accounts at EU credit institutions per MiCA requirements (≥30% at credit institutions for non-significant issuers, ≥60% for significant; remainder in highly liquid government instruments). DNB supervisory oversight of segregation. Monthly attestations published; reserve composition publicly disclosed. Reserve quality framework structurally equivalent to Circle EURC.

governance.label: Governance
governance.rationale: DNB-supervised EMI with fit-and-proper assessment of senior management and substantial shareholders. Independent EMI structure (no bank parent). Smaller operational scale than Circle or Banking Circle; governance maturity assessment limited by shorter operational track record and less public disclosure of board composition and audit arrangements relative to public-company peers.

technology.label: Technology & Custody
technology.rationale: EURQ deployed on Ethereum, Solana, and other major chains with audited smart contracts. Mint/burn controlled by Quantoz operational keys with multi-sig governance. Reserve custody at EU credit institutions per MiCA segregation rules. Smart contracts include compliance-driven freeze and blacklist functions for EU sanctions enforcement.

redemption.label: Redemption Mechanics
redemption.rationale: MiCA Article 39 par-redemption right applies. Quantoz customers redeem 1:1 against EUR bank account credits. Institutional partners (Kraken, Bitfinex) provide additional retail redemption pathways through exchange-level conversions. No redemption fees per MiCA prohibition.

disclosure.label: Public Disclosure
disclosure.rationale: Monthly third-party attestations published on Quantoz transparency portal. Whitepaper notified to DNB per MiCA Article 51. DNB receives ongoing prudential reporting. On-chain circulation observable via public blockchain. Disclosure cadence aligned with MiCA framework requirements; granularity comparable to peer EMI issuers.

reserve_details: Reserves held in segregated accounts at EU credit institutions per MiCA Articles 36-38. Composition: cash deposits at multiple EU banks plus highly liquid government securities with short residual maturity. DNB supervisory oversight of segregation arrangements. Bankruptcy-remote treatment under Dutch law for segregated e-money funds.
governance_notes: Dutch-incorporated EMI under DNB supervision. Senior management and substantial shareholders subject to DNB fit-and-proper review. Independent EMI structure without integration into bank or public-company governance frameworks. Smaller operational scale and shorter track record than Circle/Banking Circle peers; governance disclosure relatively less public.
technology_notes: Multi-chain deployment (Ethereum, Solana, additional EVM chains). Native EURQ contracts (not bridged from off-chain ledger). Mint/burn controlled by Quantoz multi-sig keys. Smart contracts include freeze and blacklist functions for EU sanctions and MiCA compliance enforcement.
redemption_notes: Par-redemption per MiCA Article 39. Direct Quantoz customer redemption 1:1 against EUR bank deposits. Exchange partnerships (Kraken, Bitfinex) provide retail redemption access. No redemption fees per MiCA prohibition.
disclosure_notes: Monthly attestations on Quantoz transparency portal. Whitepaper filed with DNB. DNB receives ongoing supervisory and prudential reporting per MiCA + EMI dual framework. On-chain circulation transparency via public blockchain.
reviewer_note: Reference case for Dutch DNB authorisation path under MiCA. Independent EMI structure (no bank parent, no public-company governance) — useful comparator showing MiCA EMT regime's diversity: bank-issuers (SG-Forge, Banking Circle), publicly-listed group EMI (Circle), and independent EMI (Quantoz) all operate under same regulatory framework with structural differences in capital base and governance depth.

name: StraitsX (XUSD)
summary: US-dollar e-money token issued by StraitsX Pte. Ltd. under MAS Major Payment Institution licence with Single-Currency Stablecoin issuance scope. Companion product to StraitsX XSGD (SGD-pegged). Same issuer, same regulatory framework, different peg — provides useful single-issuer reserve composition comparison across SGD and USD operations.

capital_adequacy.label: Capital Adequacy
capital_adequacy.rationale: Same MPI licence and capital framework as XSGD. MAS SCS Framework requires base capital SGD 1 million or 50% of annual operating expenses (whichever higher). Issuance of multiple SCS products under single MPI licence is permitted; capital adequacy assessed at entity level across combined operations. Group-level support from Fazz Financial Group.

reserve_quality.label: Reserve Quality
reserve_quality.rationale: USD reserves held in segregated accounts at MAS-licensed banks. MAS SCS Framework permits cash, cash equivalents, and short-term debt securities (≤3-month residual maturity) from sovereigns or central banks with ≥AA- credit rating. US Treasury bills qualify. Monthly attestations by independent auditor; reserve composition publicly disclosed alongside XSGD.

governance.label: Governance
governance.rationale: Same governance framework as XSGD (single issuer entity). MPI licence requires MAS fit-and-proper assessment. Multi-product operations under single regulated entity simplify governance vs multi-entity group structures (Paxos Group). Risk management framework covers both SGD and USD operations.

sources.label: MAS Payment Services Act licensee obligations

technology.label: Technology & Custody
technology.rationale: XUSD deployed on Ethereum, Polygon, Avalanche, and additional chains with audited smart contracts. Same security architecture as XSGD. Multi-sig controls for mint/burn operations; HSM-based key management. Reserve custody at MAS-licensed banks holding USD deposits.

redemption.label: Redemption Mechanics
redemption.rationale: MAS SCS Framework mandates par redemption within 5 business days of request. XUSD customers redeem 1:1 against USD bank deposits via StraitsX platform. Cross-currency conversion available between XUSD and XSGD. No redemption fees per MAS SCS rules.

disclosure.label: Public Disclosure
disclosure.rationale: Monthly third-party attestations covering both XSGD and XUSD reserve composition. Public transparency portal with real-time circulation data per token. MAS-regulated label visible on official channels. Whitepaper notified to MAS. Disclosure cadence consistent with XSGD operations.

reserve_details: USD reserves segregated at MAS-licensed banks under SCS Framework. Composition: USD cash deposits and short-dated US Treasury bills (≤3-month residual maturity) — Treasuries qualify under MAS SCS rules for AA-rated sovereign instruments. Bankruptcy-remote treatment under Singapore trust law for segregated payment account assets.
governance_notes: Single MPI licence covers both XSGD and XUSD operations under unified StraitsX governance framework. MAS supervision applies across combined operations. Multi-currency stablecoin product line under single regulated entity simplifies regulatory coordination relative to multi-entity group structures.
technology_notes: Multi-chain deployment with consistent contract architecture across XUSD and XSGD. Mint/burn under multi-sig operational controls. Smart contracts include compliance freeze and blacklist functions. Native deployment on each supported chain (not bridged).
redemption_notes: Two-tier redemption: institutional clients via direct StraitsX integration with same-day or T+1 settlement; retail via StraitsX consumer app within 5 business days per MAS SCS rules. Cross-currency conversion between XUSD and XSGD available within StraitsX platform.
disclosure_notes: Monthly attestations cover both XSGD and XUSD reserves with composition breakdown per token. Public transparency portal updated monthly. MAS receives ongoing prudential reporting at entity level.
reviewer_note: Companion product to XSGD under same StraitsX MPI licence. Useful single-issuer multi-peg comparator: identical regulatory framework, governance, and operational infrastructure applied to SGD and USD reserves separately. MAS SCS Framework permits multi-currency operation under single licence — a regulatory architecture distinct from MiCA (where each significant token may trigger separate thresholds) and from NYDFS trust charters (which similarly accommodate multiple stablecoins under single trust company).

name: Membrane Finance (EUROe)
summary: Euro-denominated e-money token issued by Membrane Finance Oy under Finnish Financial Supervisory Authority (FIN-FSA) Electronic Money Institution licence, passporting across the EEA under MiCA EMT regime. One of the earliest fully-regulated EUR stablecoins (launched February 2023, pre-MiCA implementation). Operated under Finnish EMI framework before MiCA went live; transitioned to MiCA EMT status in 2024.

capital_adequacy.label: Capital Adequacy
capital_adequacy.rationale: Membrane Finance holds Finnish EMI licence under FIN-FSA supervision per E-Money Directive 2009/110/EC, with minimum own funds EUR 350,000 plus ongoing capital proportional to outstanding e-money. MiCA Article 35 own funds requirement applies post-MiCA transition. Independent EMI structure; FIN-FSA supervision provides Nordic regulatory rigor.

reserve_quality.label: Reserve Quality
reserve_quality.rationale: Reserves held in segregated accounts at EU credit institutions per MiCA Articles 36-38. Composition: cash deposits at major EU banks plus highly liquid government securities (predominantly short-dated euro-area sovereign bonds). Monthly attestations published. FIN-FSA supervisory oversight of segregation arrangements per MiCA + EMI dual framework.

governance.label: Governance
governance.rationale: FIN-FSA-supervised EMI with fit-and-proper assessment of senior management. Finnish corporate governance baseline. Independent EMI structure without bank parent or public-company governance overlay. Operational scale modest relative to Circle EURC or bank-issuer peers; board composition and audit arrangements less publicly disclosed than larger peers.

technology.label: Technology & Custody
technology.rationale: EUROe deployed on Ethereum, Polygon, Avalanche, Solana, and additional EVM chains with audited smart contracts. Native deployment per chain (not bridged). Mint/burn controlled by Membrane operational keys with multi-sig governance. Reserve custody at EU credit institutions per MiCA segregation. Smart contracts include compliance-driven freeze and blacklist functions.

redemption.label: Redemption Mechanics
redemption.rationale: MiCA Article 39 par-redemption right applies. EUROe customers redeem 1:1 against EUR bank account credits via Membrane Finance platform. No redemption fees per MiCA prohibition. Settlement via SEPA bank rails for institutional and verified retail customers.

disclosure.label: Public Disclosure
disclosure.rationale: Monthly third-party attestations published on EUROe transparency portal. Whitepaper notified to FIN-FSA per MiCA Article 51. FIN-FSA receives ongoing prudential reporting under dual EMI + MiCA framework. On-chain circulation observable via supported blockchains. Disclosure cadence aligned with MiCA Title III requirements.

reserve_details: Reserves held in segregated accounts at EU credit institutions per MiCA Articles 36-38. Composition: cash deposits at major EU banks plus highly liquid government securities — predominantly short-dated euro-area sovereign bonds qualifying under MiCA reserve eligibility rules. Bankruptcy-remote treatment under Finnish law for segregated e-money funds; FIN-FSA supervisory oversight provides additional segregation enforcement.
governance_notes: Finnish EMI under FIN-FSA supervision. Senior management subject to fit-and-proper assessment. Independent EMI structure without integration into bank or public-company governance frameworks. Smaller operational scale than Circle/Banking Circle peers; governance disclosure follows Finnish corporate transparency norms with limited public board composition information.
technology_notes: Multi-chain native deployment across Ethereum, Polygon, Avalanche, Solana, and additional EVM chains. Mint/burn controlled by Membrane multi-sig operational keys. Smart contracts include freeze and blacklist functions for EU sanctions and MiCA compliance enforcement. Public verified contracts.
redemption_notes: Par-redemption per MiCA Article 39 via Membrane Finance platform direct integration. Settlement through SEPA rails for institutional and verified retail customers. No redemption fees. Secondary market redemption available via partner exchanges.
disclosure_notes: Monthly attestations on EUROe transparency portal with reserve composition breakdown. Whitepaper filed with FIN-FSA. FIN-FSA receives ongoing supervisory and prudential reporting under dual EMI + MiCA framework. On-chain circulation transparency via public blockchains.
reviewer_note: Reference case for early-mover Finnish/Nordic MiCA EMT path. Notable as one of the earliest fully-regulated EUR stablecoins (launched February 2023, pre-MiCA), demonstrating that the regulated EMT model is not exclusive to large established issuers. EU MiCA dataset now spans bank-issuers (SG-Forge FR, Banking Circle LU), publicly-listed group EMI (Circle FR), independent EMI (Quantoz NL), and small-scale early-mover EMI (Membrane FI) — full structural diversity within single regulatory framework.

name: Ripple USD (RLUSD)
parent: Standard Custody & Trust Company, LLC (Ripple subsidiary)
summary: US-dollar stablecoin issued by Standard Custody & Trust Company (Ripple's NYDFS limited-purpose trust company subsidiary) under New York State Department of Financial Services trust charter. Launched December 2024 with explicit dual-chain deployment on Ethereum and XRP Ledger. Fourth USD stablecoin operating under NYDFS limited-purpose trust framework alongside Paxos USDP, Gemini GUSD, and PayPal PYUSD.

capital_adequacy.label: Capital Adequacy
capital_adequacy.rationale: Standard Custody & Trust Company holds NYDFS limited-purpose trust charter subject to NY Banking Law Article III-A capital and prudential requirements. NYDFS June 2022 stablecoin guidance applies. Group-level support from Ripple Labs — Ripple Labs reported significant cash reserves and treasury holdings per past disclosures, providing structural backing to the trust subsidiary.

sources.label: NYDFS Standard Custody & Trust Company supervision

reserve_quality.label: Reserve Quality
reserve_quality.rationale: Reserves held in segregated accounts per NYDFS qualifying assets framework: US Treasury bills (≤3-month maturity), overnight reverse repos collateralised by Treasuries, and insured deposits at US banks. Monthly third-party attestations published. Bankruptcy-remote treatment under NY Banking Law trust segregation provisions — identical framework to Paxos USDP and Gemini GUSD.

governance.label: Governance
governance.rationale: NYDFS-supervised trust company governance applies — fit-and-proper assessment, ongoing examination cycle, fiduciary duties to token holders. However, Ripple Labs parent group has ongoing SEC litigation history (XRP institutional sales ruling 2023, appeals pending). Litigation overhang does not directly affect RLUSD reserves or trust company operations — Standard Custody is bankruptcy-remote — but creates group-level governance complexity not present in cleaner peer profiles.

technology.label: Technology & Custody
technology.rationale: RLUSD deployed natively on Ethereum (ERC-20) and XRP Ledger — dual-chain native deployment without bridging. Audited smart contracts on Ethereum. XRP Ledger native issuance leverages built-in token issuance primitives without smart contract complexity. Mint/burn controlled by Standard Custody operational keys. Reserve custody self-custodied as NYDFS-qualified custodian.

redemption.label: Redemption Mechanics
redemption.rationale: Direct 1:1 USD redemption available to Standard Custody account holders with KYC verification, typically T+1 settlement. NYDFS trust framework imposes redemption obligation as fiduciary duty. Banking-rail settlement infrastructure. Same structural framework as Paxos USDP and Gemini GUSD.

disclosure.label: Public Disclosure
disclosure.rationale: Monthly third-party attestations covering reserve composition and value. Reserve composition published on Ripple USD transparency portal. NYDFS receives ongoing supervisory reporting. Disclosure cadence aligned with NYDFS guidance peer standard set by Paxos USDP and Gemini GUSD.

reserve_details: 1:1 backing in NYDFS-qualifying assets: US Treasury bills with residual maturity ≤3 months, overnight reverse repos collateralised by Treasuries, and FDIC-insured deposits at US banks. Held in segregated trust accounts under NY Banking Law fiduciary segregation — bankruptcy-remote from Standard Custody general creditors and from Ripple Labs parent group. Reserve structure structurally identical to Paxos USDP and Gemini GUSD.
governance_notes: NYDFS limited-purpose trust charter for Standard Custody & Trust Company under NY Banking Law Article III-A. Fiduciary duties to token holders. Senior management subject to NYDFS fit-and-proper review at trust company level. Parent group (Ripple Labs) regulatory profile complicated by ongoing SEC litigation regarding XRP token sales — but RLUSD reserves and trust company operations are bankruptcy-remote from parent group risk.
technology_notes: Dual-chain native deployment: Ethereum (ERC-20) and XRP Ledger (native token). No bridge dependency — native issuance on each chain. Mint/burn controlled by Standard Custody multi-sig keys. Ethereum smart contracts audited and verified. XRP Ledger issuance uses native protocol primitives with built-in trust line and freeze functionality.
redemption_notes: Direct 1:1 USD redemption to Standard Custody account holders. T+1 typical settlement via banking rails. Secondary market redemption via exchanges. Cross-chain redemption: holders on either Ethereum or XRP Ledger can redeem to USD through Standard Custody platform.
disclosure_notes: Monthly attestations on Ripple USD transparency portal with reserve composition breakdown. NYDFS receives ongoing supervisory and prudential reporting. Disclosure cadence consistent with NYDFS trust peer group (USDP, GUSD, PYUSD).
reviewer_note: Fourth USD stablecoin operationalised under NYDFS limited-purpose trust framework (alongside USDP, GUSD, PYUSD). Distinguishing feature: dual-chain native deployment on Ethereum and XRP Ledger — most peers deploy on Ethereum primarily. NYDFS framework provides clean structural assessment template; group-level Ripple Labs litigation overhang documented as governance-yellow without affecting trust company reserve assessment. Useful as fourth data point in NYDFS trust comparator set.

name: Diem (formerly Libra)
parent: Diem Association (formerly Libra Association) / Meta Platforms (initiator)
summary: Stablecoin project initiated by Meta (then Facebook) in June 2019, originally as a multi-currency basket-backed token, repivoted to single-currency design in 2020. Despite well-resourced consortium governance and explicit regulatory engagement, the project failed to achieve authorisation under any major framework. Diem Association wound down in January 2022; remaining technology and reserves sold to Silvergate Capital (subsequently failed during 2023 US banking crisis). Reference case for failed regulatory authorisation despite substantial institutional sponsorship.

capital_adequacy.label: Capital Adequacy
capital_adequacy.rationale: Diem Association reported substantial committed capital from consortium members at peak ($1.65B raised across members). However, governance and reserve obligations were never operationalised under a binding supervisor with capital adequacy enforcement. Capital adequacy assessment is moot for a withdrawn issuer; status reflects regime gap rather than identified deficiency at design stage.

reserve_quality.label: Reserve Quality
reserve_quality.rationale: Design intended 1:1 backing in a basket of low-volatility assets (initial design) or single-currency cash plus short-dated government debt (2020 revised design). Reserves never operationalised at scale. Diem reserve framework was never tested under live conditions; assessment is structurally moot.

sources.label: BIS Project Helvetia commentary on Libra

governance.label: Governance
governance.rationale: Despite Geneva-based Diem Association structure with named consortium members and documented governance bylaws, the project faced sustained regulatory and political opposition globally. G7 statement (2019) and FSB high-level recommendations effectively foreclosed authorisation paths. US Federal Reserve, Treasury, and Congressional opposition made Federal Reserve master account access infeasible. Multiple consortium members (Visa, Mastercard, PayPal, Stripe, eBay) withdrew during 2019-2020. Governance structure proved insufficient to overcome jurisdiction-by-jurisdiction regulatory resistance.

sources.label: G7 working group report on stablecoins (Oct 2019)
FSB high-level recommendations on global stablecoins (2020)

technology.label: Technology & Custody
technology.rationale: Diem developed proprietary Move-based blockchain (later open-sourced; technology lives on in Aptos and Sui as derivative chains). Custody arrangements with Silvergate Bank operationalised but limited live deployment. Technical architecture was advanced relative to peers but never operationally validated at retail scale under regulated conditions.

redemption.label: Redemption Mechanics
redemption.rationale: Redemption mechanism never operationalised. Designs included direct redemption to authorised resellers (institutional) with retail redemption via wallet providers (Novi/Calibra). Assessment is structurally moot given non-launch.

disclosure.label: Public Disclosure
disclosure.rationale: Diem Association published whitepapers (v1 2019, v2 2020), governance documents, technical specifications, and periodic public updates — disclosure baseline was high relative to typical pre-launch stablecoin projects. Member roster and contributions publicly disclosed. However, given non-operationalisation, no live reserve attestation or supervisory reporting framework existed.

reserve_details: Designed but not operationalised: initial multi-currency basket model (USD, EUR, GBP, JPY, SGD) — abandoned 2020. Revised single-currency design (Diem USD, Diem EUR etc., each 1:1 backed by cash and short-dated government securities of respective currency). Custody arrangements with Silvergate Bank operationalised in pre-launch phase. Reserves never deployed at retail scale.
governance_notes: Diem Association — Geneva-incorporated non-profit consortium with rotating board, named founding members, and documented governance bylaws. Initial 28 members at launch (2019) declined to 21 by 2020 after high-profile withdrawals (Visa, Mastercard, PayPal, Stripe, eBay, Mercado Pago). G7 and FSB regulatory engagement extensive; US Federal Reserve, Treasury, and Congressional opposition decisive. Wind-down formally announced January 31, 2022; remaining IP and reserves sold to Silvergate Capital for $182M.
technology_notes: Diem blockchain built on proprietary Move smart contract language — open-sourced post-wind-down. Move language and Diem architecture forked into Aptos and Sui blockchain projects (both launched 2022). Custody integration with Silvergate Bank operationalised. Technical architecture was advanced; regulatory rather than technical factors caused project failure.
redemption_notes: Designed but not operationalised: institutional authorised resellers would mint/burn against fiat deposits with Diem Association; retail users would redeem via wallet providers (Novi/Calibra). Mechanism never tested under live conditions.
disclosure_notes: Pre-launch disclosure baseline was high: detailed whitepapers, governance documents, member rosters, technical specifications publicly available throughout project lifetime. Final wind-down statement published January 2022. No operational reserve attestation or supervisory disclosure framework given non-launch.

citations.label: G7 Working Group Report on Stablecoins (Oct 2019)
Silvergate acquisition of Diem assets announcement (Jan 2022)

reviewer_note: Most important withdrawn-stablecoin case study. Demonstrates that institutional sponsorship and technical sophistication are insufficient absent regulatory authorisation pathway. Particularly relevant context for understanding why post-2022 stablecoin regulatory frameworks (MiCA, MAS SCS, HK Ordinance, NYDFS guidance) emphasise pre-authorisation supervisory engagement and explicit license requirements. Many SARM signals marked 'gray' (insufficient data) reflect non-operationalisation rather than substantive deficiency — Diem withdrawal was a regulatory outcome, not a structural failure of the specific design proposed. Closely connected to Lessons Learned postmortem case studies (TerraUSD, Iron Finance) as taxonomy of stablecoin failure modes.

name: Bank of China (Hong Kong)
parent: Bank of China (Hong Kong) Limited (HKEX: 2388 — BOC Hong Kong Holdings)
summary: Hong Kong-dollar stablecoin proposal by Bank of China (Hong Kong) — the third of Hong Kong's three banknote-issuing banks (alongside HSBC and Standard Chartered). Among the reported pool of 36 first-window applicants under the Stablecoins Ordinance Cap. 649. Not in the first batch of licences announced 10 April 2026 (HSBC and Anchorpoint Financial); status under review for subsequent batches. As one of three statutory HKD banknote issuers, BOCHK represents the most institutionally significant pending applicant.

capital_adequacy.label: Capital Adequacy
capital_adequacy.rationale: BOC Hong Kong operates under HKMA banking supervision with full Basel III capital adequacy requirements. As one of three HKD banknote-issuing banks (statutory note-issuance privilege), BOCHK occupies a category of institutional significance distinct from non-bank issuers. HKEX-listed (stock code 2388) with public-company capital disclosures. Group support from Bank of China (Beijing) — a Chinese G-SIB.

sources.label: BOC Hong Kong Holdings annual reports

reserve_quality.label: Reserve Quality
reserve_quality.rationale: Specific reserve composition design not publicly disclosed at application stage. Stablecoins Ordinance Cap. 649 requires 1:1 backing in high-quality liquid assets held at HKMA-authorised institutions. As a HKMA-supervised commercial bank, BOCHK has direct access to optimal reserve quality infrastructure (HKMA deposits, Exchange Fund Bills). Operational reserve framework would inherit from banking treasury operations once licensed.

governance.label: Governance
governance.rationale: Full HKMA banking supervision under Banking Ordinance Cap. 155. Bank-grade governance: independent directors, audit committee, board risk committee. HKEX-listed public company with Listing Rules disclosure obligations. Group oversight via Bank of China Limited (Beijing) — a Chinese state-owned G-SIB with broad regulatory footprint. Senior management subject to HKMA fit-and-proper assessment.

technology.label: Technology & Custody
technology.rationale: BOCHK operates mature banking technology infrastructure with established custody and settlement capabilities. Specific stablecoin smart contract architecture and blockchain deployment not publicly disclosed at application stage. Banking-grade key management and HSM infrastructure expected. Technical implementation would inherit from existing institutional digital asset and trade finance pilots.

sources.label: BOC Hong Kong digital banking infrastructure

redemption.label: Redemption Mechanics
redemption.rationale: Redemption design not publicly disclosed at application stage. As a HKD banknote-issuing bank, BOCHK has unique structural redemption capability via direct access to HKMA Exchange Fund and Real Time Gross Settlement (RTGS) systems. Stablecoins Ordinance requires par-redemption framework once licensed. Operational redemption framework moot pending authorisation.

disclosure.label: Public Disclosure
disclosure.rationale: HKEX-listed public company subject to Listing Rules disclosure obligations: interim and annual reports, continuing disclosure of material events. HKMA banking supervision adds prudential reporting cadence. Stablecoin-specific public attestation framework would be operationalised post-licensure per HKMA requirements.

sources.label: BOC Hong Kong Holdings HKEX filings

reserve_details: Reserve composition design not publicly disclosed at application stage. As HKMA-supervised commercial bank with HKD banknote-issuance privilege, BOCHK has direct access to optimal HKD reserve infrastructure: HKMA settlement accounts, Exchange Fund Bills and Notes, and segregated trust accounts within bank balance sheet structure. Stablecoins Ordinance Cap. 649 requires 1:1 backing in high-quality liquid assets at HKMA-authorised institutions — a requirement BOCHK would naturally satisfy through internal treasury operations.
governance_notes: Full HKMA bank supervision under Banking Ordinance Cap. 155. HKEX-listed (stock code 2388, BOC Hong Kong Holdings). Independent board members, audit committee, board risk committee under HKMA Supervisory Policy Manual. Group oversight via Bank of China Limited (Beijing) — Chinese state-owned G-SIB. Senior management subject to HKMA fit-and-proper assessment under Banking Ordinance.
technology_notes: Banking-grade infrastructure with established custody, settlement, and payment processing capabilities. Specific stablecoin smart contract architecture not publicly disclosed. Likely to leverage HKMA Project mBridge / Ensemble pilot experience for cross-border and tokenised deposit interoperability. HSM-based key management and bank-standard cybersecurity controls expected.
redemption_notes: Structural redemption advantage as HKD banknote issuer: direct access to HKMA Exchange Fund settlement, HKD RTGS rails, and Currency Board operating mechanism. Stablecoins Ordinance requires par-redemption infrastructure once licensed; design specifics not yet public.
disclosure_notes: HKEX Listing Rules disclosure regime provides baseline transparency. HKMA banking supervision adds prudential reporting. Stablecoin-specific disclosure framework to be operationalised post-authorisation per HKMA requirements (monthly reserve attestations, public composition disclosure).
reviewer_note: Third of Hong Kong's three HKD banknote-issuing banks (alongside HSBC, Standard Chartered consortium via Anchorpoint Financial). Not in HKMA first batch (April 2026) but widely reported in the 36-applicant pool. Status as banknote issuer with direct HKMA settlement infrastructure access provides structural advantage in subsequent licensure rounds. Useful comparator within HK dataset for analysing how the three banknote-issuing banks navigate the stablecoin regime differently — HSBC standalone, Standard Chartered consortium, BOCHK pending. Several SARM signals marked 'gray' reflect pre-authorisation status rather than substantive deficiency.

name: WisdomTree (WUSD)
parent: WisdomTree Digital Trust Company, LLC (WisdomTree, Inc. — NYSE: WT)
summary: US-dollar stablecoin issued by WisdomTree Digital Trust Company under New York State Department of Financial Services limited-purpose trust charter. Sister product to WisdomTree Prime's broader tokenized fund and digital asset platform. Parent WisdomTree, Inc. is a publicly-listed traditional asset manager (NYSE: WT) with $100B+ AUM in ETF and fund products — distinct origin story from crypto-native peer issuers.

capital_adequacy.label: Capital Adequacy
capital_adequacy.rationale: WisdomTree Digital Trust Company holds NYDFS limited-purpose trust charter under NY Banking Law Article III-A capital requirements. NYDFS June 2022 stablecoin guidance applies. Group-level support from WisdomTree, Inc. — a NYSE-listed traditional asset manager with substantial corporate capital, SEC-registered fund operations, and established regulatory infrastructure. Capital adequacy framework structurally robust.

reserve_quality.label: Reserve Quality
reserve_quality.rationale: Reserves held per NYDFS qualifying assets framework: US Treasury bills (≤3-month maturity), overnight reverse repos collateralised by Treasuries, and insured deposits at US banks. Monthly third-party attestations published. Bankruptcy-remote trust segregation under NY Banking Law. WisdomTree's traditional asset management expertise provides institutional-grade treasury operations.

governance.label: Governance
governance.rationale: NYDFS-supervised trust company governance with fiduciary duties to token holders. Public-company parent (WisdomTree, Inc., NYSE: WT) provides additional governance layer: SEC reporting, independent board, audit committee, Sarbanes-Oxley controls. Senior management subject to NYDFS fit-and-proper review. Traditional asset management governance standards applied to stablecoin operations.

technology.label: Technology & Custody
technology.rationale: WUSD deployed on Ethereum and Stellar with audited smart contracts. Mint/burn controlled by WisdomTree Digital Trust operational keys with multi-sig governance. Reserve custody self-custodied as NYDFS-qualified custodian. Smart contracts include compliance freeze and blacklist functions. Integration with WisdomTree Prime app for retail wallet access.

redemption.label: Redemption Mechanics
redemption.rationale: Direct 1:1 USD redemption available to WisdomTree Prime account holders with KYC verification. T+1 typical settlement via banking rails. NYDFS trust framework imposes redemption obligation as fiduciary duty. Same structural framework as other NYDFS trust stablecoins (USDP, GUSD, RLUSD).

disclosure.label: Public Disclosure
disclosure.rationale: Monthly third-party attestations covering reserve composition. NYDFS receives ongoing supervisory reporting. Parent WisdomTree, Inc. public-company SEC disclosures (10-K, 10-Q, 8-K) provide additional group-level transparency. Disclosure cadence consistent with NYDFS trust peer standards.

reserve_details: 1:1 backing in NYDFS-qualifying assets: US Treasury bills with residual maturity ≤3 months, overnight reverse repos collateralised by Treasuries, and FDIC-insured deposits at US banks. Held in segregated trust accounts under NY Banking Law fiduciary segregation — bankruptcy-remote from WisdomTree Digital Trust general creditors and from WisdomTree, Inc. parent. Reserve structure structurally identical to peer NYDFS trust stablecoins.
governance_notes: NYDFS limited-purpose trust charter for WisdomTree Digital Trust Company. Public-company parent (NYSE: WT) provides additional layer of governance: SEC reporting, Sarbanes-Oxley controls, independent board, audit committee. Traditional asset management governance heritage (~$100B AUM across ETF and fund products) brings institutional rigor distinct from crypto-native peer issuers.
technology_notes: WUSD issued natively on Ethereum and Stellar. Mint/burn controlled by WisdomTree Digital Trust multi-sig keys. Smart contract supports compliance-driven freeze and blacklist functions. Retail-facing wallet integration via WisdomTree Prime mobile app — a fully integrated tokenized finance platform spanning stablecoins, tokenized funds, and digital asset trading.
redemption_notes: Direct 1:1 USD redemption to WisdomTree Prime account holders with KYC verification. T+1 settlement via WisdomTree Digital Trust banking relationships. Secondary market redemption via exchanges with smaller liquidity depth than larger peer stablecoins (USDC, USDP).
disclosure_notes: Monthly attestations on WUSD transparency portal with reserve composition. NYDFS ongoing supervisory reporting. WisdomTree, Inc. SEC filings (10-K, 10-Q) provide audited group financials and stablecoin operations disclosure as material business segment. Dual-layer transparency: trust company level + public-company level.

citations.label: WisdomTree Digital Trust Company NYDFS charter

reviewer_note: Fifth USD stablecoin under NYDFS limited-purpose trust framework (USDP, GUSD, PYUSD, RLUSD, WUSD). Distinguishing feature: parent is NYSE-listed traditional asset manager (WisdomTree, Inc.), representing the largest TradFi entry into regulated USD stablecoins to date. Useful comparator showing that NYDFS framework accommodates issuers ranging from crypto-native (Paxos, Gemini), platform-issuer split (PayPal/Paxos), to traditional asset management (WisdomTree). NYDFS trust template is the most established multi-issuer regulated stablecoin framework globally.

name: SBI VC Trade (JPYS)
parent: SBI VC Trade Co., Ltd. (SBI Holdings)
summary: Japanese-yen stablecoin proposal by SBI VC Trade, the crypto asset exchange subsidiary of SBI Holdings (one of Japan's largest online financial services groups). Application pending under the revised Payment Services Act framework. SBI Group has publicly committed to JPY stablecoin issuance across multiple business lines including remittances, brokerage settlement, and Web3 integration; specific issuance route (Type II Fund Transfer Service Provider vs trust-type via Progmat) subject to ongoing regulatory engagement.

capital_adequacy.label: Capital Adequacy
capital_adequacy.rationale: SBI VC Trade operates as registered crypto asset exchange under JVCEA self-regulation and FSA supervision. Parent SBI Holdings is TSE-listed (stock code 8473) with substantial group capital across banking (SBI Sumishin Net Bank), securities (SBI Securities), and insurance subsidiaries. Stablecoin issuance under revised PSA would inherit banking-equivalent capital requirements via FTSP or trust-bank issuance route.

reserve_quality.label: Reserve Quality
reserve_quality.rationale: Specific reserve composition design not publicly disclosed at application stage. Revised PSA bank/trust route would require 100% backing in qualifying liquid assets with trust segregation under Japanese trust law. FTSP route would impose similar 100% backing requirement. Operational reserve framework moot pending licensure approval.

governance.label: Governance
governance.rationale: SBI VC Trade governance under JVCEA and FSA crypto asset exchange supervision. Group oversight via SBI Holdings — TSE-listed public company with conglomerate governance structure. Multi-business-line group structure adds complexity; stablecoin-specific governance arrangements not yet operationalised. Senior management subject to FSA fit-and-proper review at exchange level; banking-equivalent review would apply post-FTSP or trust route approval.

technology.label: Technology & Custody
technology.rationale: SBI VC Trade operates regulated crypto asset exchange infrastructure with operational track record. Specific stablecoin smart contract architecture and chain deployment plan not publicly disclosed. Group expertise in financial technology, brokerage settlement systems, and digital asset custody. Technical implementation would inherit from existing exchange infrastructure and group fintech capabilities.

redemption.label: Redemption Mechanics
redemption.rationale: Redemption design not publicly disclosed at application stage. Revised PSA bank/trust or FTSP routes both require par-redemption framework. SBI Group's existing banking infrastructure (SBI Sumishin Net Bank) provides natural redemption rail capability. Operational redemption framework moot pending authorisation.

disclosure.label: Public Disclosure
disclosure.rationale: SBI Holdings TSE-listed disclosure regime provides baseline group transparency. Crypto asset exchange disclosures under JVCEA framework. Stablecoin-specific public attestation framework not yet operationalised given pre-authorisation status. Group-level financial transparency strong; entity-level stablecoin transparency to be developed.

reserve_details: Reserve composition design not publicly disclosed at application stage. Revised PSA imposes 100% backing requirement in qualifying liquid assets under either trust-bank issuance route (segregated trust accounts at licensed trust banks) or Type II FTSP route (asset preservation requirements). SBI Group has access to SBI Sumishin Net Bank for treasury operations if pursuing bank-issuance pathway.
governance_notes: SBI VC Trade — crypto asset exchange subsidiary under SBI Holdings (TSE-listed conglomerate). Multi-business-line group structure includes banking (SBI Sumishin Net Bank), securities (SBI Securities), insurance, and asset management subsidiaries. Stablecoin operations governance arrangement subject to which entity becomes licensed issuer. FSA fit-and-proper review at relevant entity level upon licensure.
technology_notes: Crypto exchange infrastructure with operational track record. SBI Group has invested in multiple blockchain and digital asset initiatives including Ripple partnership for cross-border remittances. Specific stablecoin issuance technology (chains, custody, mint/burn architecture) not yet public.
redemption_notes: Hypothetical only — pre-authorisation. Revised PSA par-redemption requirement applies once licensed. SBI Group's existing banking infrastructure (SBI Sumishin Net Bank) provides natural redemption rail capability under bank-issuance route.
disclosure_notes: SBI Holdings TSE-listed disclosure framework provides baseline transparency. Crypto exchange operations subject to JVCEA self-regulatory disclosure standards. Stablecoin-specific transparency framework to be operationalised post-authorisation per FSA requirements.
reviewer_note: Reference case for Japan's conglomerate-fintech stablecoin entry pathway. Distinct from bank-issuance (GMO Aozora, Progmat infrastructure) and prepaid-fintech (JPYC) archetypes — SBI Group represents the multi-business-line financial conglomerate model. Application currently under review; eventual issuance entity and route (FTSP vs trust-bank via Progmat) not yet finalised in public disclosures. Several SARM signals marked 'gray' reflect pre-authorisation status.
